revision.comment: [[WP:AES|←]]新しいページ: 「{{神社 |名称 = 人魚神社 |画像 = {{画像募集中|cat=八重山郡}} |所在地 = [[沖縄県]][[八重山郡]][[竹富町]]新城<ref>{{cite web|url=https://jinja-bukkaku.net/detail.htm?jbId=116279|title=人魚神社|publisher=神社がいいね・お寺がいいね|accessdate=2023-5-5}}</ref> |位置 = |祭神 = [[ジュゴン]]（ザヌ） |社格 = |創建 = 不詳 |本殿 = |別名 = |例祭 = |神事 = }} '''人魚神社'''…」
revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = 人魚神社 |画像 = {{画像募集中|cat=八重山郡}} |所在地 = [[沖縄県]][[八重山郡]][[竹富町]]新城<ref>{{cite web|url=https://jinja-bukkaku.net/detail.htm?jbId=116279|title=人魚神社|publisher=神社がいいね・お寺がいいね|accessdate=2023-5-5}}</ref> |位置 = |祭神 = [[ジュゴン]]（ザヌ） |社格 = |創建 = 不詳 |本殿 = |別名 = |例祭 = |神事 = }} '''人魚神社'''（にんぎょじんじゃ）は、[[沖縄県]][[竹富町]]の[[新城島]]（上地島）に鎮座する[[神社]]。別名は'''東御願'''（あーるうがん）。
== 歴史 == 創建年は不詳。かつて人魚神社が鎮座する[[新城島]]（上地島）の食料として養ってくれた[[ジュゴン]]（ザヌ）への供養として創建されたとされる<ref>{{cite web|url=https://ishigaki-activity.com/articles/panari-history|title=パナリ島を観光する際の注意点について（歴史など）|publisher=Wow!石垣島アクティビティ|accessdate=2023-5-5}}</ref>。なお、人魚神社は日本最南端の神社ともされているが、[[神社本庁]]に属す神社の最南端は[[宮古神社]]である<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.buccyake-kojiki.com/archives/1059312919.html|title=パナリ島を観光する際の注意点について（歴史など）|publisher=神社と古事記|accessdate=2023-5-5}}</ref>。
人魚神社は島の聖域とされているため、現在は一般の立ち入りは禁止となっている。また新城島には人魚神社のほかにも[[美嶽神社]]があるが、人魚神社と同様に一般の立ち入りは禁止とされている。
== 祭神 == * [[ジュゴン]]（ザヌ）
== 交通 == * [[上地港]]より徒歩で鳥居前まで行けるが、その先は立ち入り禁止なっている。
== 脚注 == <references />
{{Normdaten}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:にんきよしんしや}} [[Category:沖縄県の神社]] [[Category:竹富町の建築物]] [[Category:竹富町の歴史]]

{{神社 |名称 = 人魚神社 |画像 = {{画像募集中|cat=八重山郡}} |所在地 = [[沖縄県]][[八重山郡]][[竹富町]]新城<ref>{{cite web|url=https://jinja-bukkaku.net/detail.htm?jbId=116279|title=人魚神社|publisher=神社がいいね・お寺がいいね|accessdate=2023-5-5}}</ref> |位置 = |祭神 = [[ジュゴン]]（ザヌ） |社格 = |創建 = 不詳 |本殿 = |別名 = |例祭 = |神事 = }} '''人魚神社'''（にんぎょじんじゃ）は、[[沖縄県]][[竹富町]]の[[新城島]]（上地島）に鎮座する[[神社]]。正式名は'''東御願'''（あーるうがん）といい、[[琉球神道]]における[[御嶽 (沖縄)|御嶽]]である。別称は'''磯御嶽'''（いしょーうがん）。
== 歴史 == 創建年は不詳。かつて人魚神社が鎮座する[[新城島]]（上地島）の食料として養ってくれた[[ジュゴン]]（ザヌ）への供養として創建されたとされる<ref>{{cite web|url=https://ishigaki-activity.com/articles/panari-history|title=パナリ島を観光する際の注意点について（歴史など）|publisher=Wow!石垣島アクティビティ|accessdate=2023-5-5}}</ref>。なお、人魚神社は日本最南端の神社ともされているが、[[神社本庁]]に属す神社の最南端は[[宮古神社]]である<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.buccyake-kojiki.com/archives/1059312919.html|title=パナリ島を観光する際の注意点について（歴史など）|publisher=神社と古事記|accessdate=2023-5-5}}</ref>。
人魚神社は島の聖域とされているため、現在は一般の立ち入りは禁止となっている。また新城島には人魚神社のほかにも[[美嶽神社]]があるが、人魚神社と同様に一般の立ち入りは禁止とされている。
== 祭神 == * [[ジュゴン]]（ザヌ）
== 交通 == * [[上地港]]より徒歩で鳥居前まで行けるが、その先は立ち入り禁止なっている。
== 脚注 == <references />
{{Normdaten}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:にんきよしんしや}} [[Category:沖縄県の神社]] [[Category:竹富町の建築物]] [[Category:竹富町の歴史]]

{{神社 |名称 = 人魚神社 |画像 = {{画像募集中|cat=八重山郡}} |所在地 = [[沖縄県]][[八重山郡]][[竹富町]]新城<ref>{{cite web|url=https://jinja-bukkaku.net/detail.htm?jbId=116279|title=人魚神社|publisher=神社がいいね・お寺がいいね|accessdate=2023-5-5}}</ref> |位置 = |祭神 = [[ジュゴン]]（ザヌ） |社格 = |創建 = 不詳 |本殿 = |別名 = |例祭 = |神事 = }} '''人魚神社'''（にんぎょじんじゃ）は、[[沖縄県]][[竹富町]]の[[新城島]]（上地島）に鎮座する[[神社]]。正式名は'''東御嶽'''（あーるうがん）といい、[[琉球神道]]における[[御嶽 (沖縄)|御嶽]]である。別称は'''磯御嶽'''（いしょーうがん）。
== 歴史 == 創建年は不詳。かつて人魚神社が鎮座する[[新城島]]（上地島）の食料として養ってくれた[[ジュゴン]]（ザヌ）への供養として創建されたとされる<ref>{{cite web|url=https://ishigaki-activity.com/articles/panari-history|title=パナリ島を観光する際の注意点について（歴史など）|publisher=Wow!石垣島アクティビティ|accessdate=2023-5-5}}</ref>。なお、人魚神社は日本最南端の神社ともされているが、[[神社本庁]]に属す神社の最南端は[[宮古神社]]である<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.buccyake-kojiki.com/archives/1059312919.html|title=パナリ島を観光する際の注意点について（歴史など）|publisher=神社と古事記|accessdate=2023-5-5}}</ref>。
人魚神社は島の聖域とされているため、現在は一般の立ち入りは禁止となっている。また新城島には人魚神社のほかにも[[美嶽神社]]があるが、人魚神社と同様に一般の立ち入りは禁止とされている。
== 祭神 == * [[ジュゴン]]（ザヌ）
== 交通 == * [[上地港]]より徒歩で鳥居前まで行けるが、その先は立ち入り禁止なっている。
== 脚注 == <references />
{{Normdaten}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:にんきよしんしや}} [[Category:沖縄県の神社]] [[Category:竹富町の建築物]] [[Category:竹富町の歴史]]

{{改名提案|東御嶽 (竹富町新城上地)|date=2023年5月}} '''人魚神社'''（にんぎょじんじゃ）又は'''東御嶽'''（あーるうがん、あーりぃうがん<ref name="okinawastory">{{cite web | title = 新城島 | url = https://www.okinawastory.jp/spot/1304 | work = おきなわ物語 | publisher = 一般財団法人沖縄観光コンベンションビューロー | accessdate = 2023-05-14 | archiveurl = https://web.archive.org/web/20221128013234/https://www.okinawastory.jp/spot/1304 | archivedate = 2022-11-28 }}</ref>）は、[[沖縄県]][[竹富町]]の[[新城島]]（上地島）にある[[御嶽 (沖縄)|御嶽]]。'''磯御嶽'''（いしゃーうがん、いしょーうがん）、'''グスクウガン'''{{Sfn|町史|2013|p=412}}（クースクウガン<ref name="hesiki_1975">{{cite journal|和書 | title = 新城島のアカムタ・クロムタ祭祀 : 上地のウフプール覚書 | author = 平敷令治 | url = http://hdl.handle.net/2308/1372 | journal = 沖縄国際大学文学部紀要 社会学科篇 | issn = 02870681 | publisher = 沖縄国際大学 | year = 1975 | month = 03 | volume = 3 | issue = 1 | pages = 55-69 }}</ref>）、'''ザンヌオン'''<ref name="shojiten">{{cite book|和書 | title = 八重山ジャンルごと小事典 | page = 91 | author = 崎原恒新 | publisher = [[ボーダーインク]] | date = 1999-08-01 }}</ref>とも呼ばれる。人魚神社という通称で呼ばれることもあるが、[[神社]]ではない<ref>{{cite encyclopedia | language = ja | title = 新城島 | encyclopedia= 角川日本地名大辞典 47 沖縄県 | author = 角川日本地名大辞典編纂委員会 | date= 1986-07-08 | publisher= 角川書店 | page = 130 }}</ref>。
== 概要 == 新城島を構成する2島のうち、北側に位置する上地島には4つの御嶽がある。東御嶽はそのうち最も歴史が古く、美御嶽（ナハウガン）次ぐ聖地とされる御嶽である。上地島唯一の集落の東（アール）に位置し、[[龍宮]]の神を祀る{{Sfn|町史|2013|p=412}}<ref name="hesiki_1975" />。御嶽は岩石の台地上にあり、拝所の裏には石垣で囲まれた屋敷跡がある{{Sfn|町史|2013|p=414}}。周辺からは[[パナリ焼き]]が出土する<ref name="shojiten" />。
御嶽に続く道には「立ち入り禁止」の札が掛かっており、氏子以外は鳥居から先に立ち入ることはできない<ref name="okinawatimes_1999-12-18">{{cite news | title = 島を往く・船人くらし(8) 新城島（竹富町） | url = http://www.okinawatimes.co.jp/spe/yuku991218.html | newspaper = 沖縄タイムス | date = 1999-12-18 | archiveurl = https://web.archive.org/web/20000901000807/http://www.okinawatimes.co.jp/spe/yuku991218.html | archivedate = 2000-09-01 }}</ref>。
== 歴史 == 島の元御嶽（ムトゥウガン）とも呼ばれる御嶽で、上地島に人が住むようになった当初に創建されたと伝えられる{{Sfn|町史|2013|p=414}}。上地島の歴史には不詳な点が多いが、[[1477年]]に[[朝鮮]]・[[済州島]]の漂流民3人が[[与那国島]]に漂着し、島伝いに送還された際に新城島の記録を残しており、その頃には住民が定住していたことが分かる{{Sfn|町史|2013|pp=97-101}}。
[[琉球王国|琉球王府]]により[[人頭税]]が課せられた時代には、水が乏しく農作物が育たない新城島には[[ジュゴン]]（ザン、ザヌ）の干肉の貢納が命じられた。この御嶽には捕獲したジュゴンの頭骨が奉納され、ジュゴンを含む豊漁が祈願された{{Sfn|町史|2013|p=415}}<ref name="okinawastory" /><ref name="shojiten" />。かつて御嶽にはジュゴンの頭骨が山積みされていた{{Sfn|町史|2013|p=34}}が、盗まれる等してその数は減少している<ref name="okinawatimes_1999-12-18" />。「ザンヌオン」という別名は「ジュゴンの御嶽」という意味であり、「磯御嶽」の「磯」には「海」という意味もある{{Sfn|町史|2013|p=414}}<ref name="hesiki_1975" />。
== 祭祀 == 島の三大行事である豊年祭（ブル）、節祭（シ<sub>ヰ</sub>チ<sub>ヰ</sub>）、結願祭（キチュガン）で、祈願の対象となる{{Sfn|町史|2013|p=415}}。
神司{{Efn|祭祀を司る女性神職。}}は安里家（アールアサデー）、カマンガー{{Efn|神司を補佐する男性神職。}}は新家（アーラエー）である{{Sfn|町史|2013|p=412}}が、2013年時点で神司は不在である{{Sfn|町史|2013|p=415}}。
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} === 注釈 === {{Notelist}} === 出典 === {{Reflist|2}}
== 参考文献 == * {{cite book|和書 | title = 竹富町史 第五巻 新城島 | editor = 竹富町史編集委員会 | date = 2013-11-30 | ref = {{SfnRef|町史|2013}} }}
== 関連項目 == * [[アカマタ・クロマタ]]
{{Normdaten}} {{Reli-stub}} {{Pref-stub|pref=沖縄県}} {{DEFAULTSORT:にんきよしんしや}} [[Category:沖縄県の宗教施設]] [[Category:琉球の信仰]] [[Category:竹富町の建築物]] [[Category:竹富町の歴史]]

{{改名提案|東御嶽 (竹富町新城上地)|date=2023年5月}} '''人魚神社'''（にんぎょじんじゃ）又は'''東御嶽'''（あーるうがん、あーりぃうがん<ref name="okinawastory">{{cite web | title = 新城島 | url = https://www.okinawastory.jp/spot/1304 | work = おきなわ物語 | publisher = 一般財団法人沖縄観光コンベンションビューロー | accessdate = 2023-05-14 | archiveurl = https://web.archive.org/web/20221128013234/https://www.okinawastory.jp/spot/1304 | archivedate = 2022-11-28 }}</ref>）は、[[沖縄県]][[竹富町]]の[[新城島]]（上地島）にある[[御嶽 (沖縄)|御嶽]]。'''磯御嶽'''（いしゃーうがん、いしょーうがん）、'''グスクウガン'''{{Sfn|町史|2013|p=412}}（クースクウガン<ref name="hesiki_1975">{{cite journal|和書 | title = 新城島のアカムタ・クロムタ祭祀 : 上地のウフプール覚書 | author = 平敷令治 | url = http://hdl.handle.net/2308/1372 | journal = 沖縄国際大学文学部紀要 社会学科篇 | issn = 02870681 | publisher = 沖縄国際大学 | year = 1975 | month = 03 | volume = 3 | issue = 1 | pages = 55-69 }}</ref>）、'''ザンヌオン'''<ref name="shojiten">{{cite book|和書 | title = 八重山ジャンルごと小事典 | page = 91 | author = 崎原恒新 | publisher = [[ボーダーインク]] | date = 1999-08-01 }}</ref>とも呼ばれる。人魚神社という通称で呼ばれることもあるが、[[神社]]ではない<ref>{{cite encyclopedia | language = ja | title = 新城島 | encyclopedia= 角川日本地名大辞典 47 沖縄県 | author = 角川日本地名大辞典編纂委員会 | date= 1986-07-08 | publisher= 角川書店 | page = 130 }}</ref>。

== 歴史 == 島の元御嶽（ムトゥウガン）とも呼ばれる御嶽で、上地島に人が住むようになった当初に創建されたと伝えられる{{Sfn|町史|2013|p=414}}。上地島の歴史には不詳な点が多いが、[[1477年]]に[[朝鮮]]・[[済州島]]の漂流民3人が[[与那国島]]に漂着し、島伝いに送還された際に新城島の記録を残しており、その頃には住民が定住していたことが分かる{{Sfn|町史|2013|pp=97-101}}。
[[琉球王国|琉球王府]]により[[人頭税]]が課せられた時代には、水が乏しく農作物が育たない新城島には[[ジュゴン]]（ザン、ザヌ）の干肉の貢納が命じられた。この御嶽には捕獲したジュゴンの頭骨が奉納され、ジュゴンを含む豊漁が祈願された{{Sfn|町史|2013|p=415}}<ref name="okinawastory" /><ref name="shojiten" />。かつて御嶽にはジュゴンの頭骨が山積みされていた{{Sfn|町史|2013|p=34}}が、盗まれる等してその数は減少している<ref name="okinawatimes_1999-12-18" />。「ザンヌオン」という別名は「ジュゴンの御嶽」という意味であり、「磯御嶽」の「磯」には「海」という意味もある{{Sfn|町史|2013|p=414}}<ref name="hesiki_1975" />。

revision.comment: Paipateroma がページ「[[人魚神社]]」を「[[東御嶽 (竹富町新城上地)]]」に移動しました: [[Wikipedia:改名提案|改名提案]]による
revision.text:
{{改名提案|東御嶽 (竹富町新城上地)|date=2023年5月}} '''人魚神社'''（にんぎょじんじゃ）又は'''東御嶽'''（あーるうがん、あーりぃうがん<ref name="okinawastory">{{cite web | title = 新城島 | url = https://www.okinawastory.jp/spot/1304 | work = おきなわ物語 | publisher = 一般財団法人沖縄観光コンベンションビューロー | accessdate = 2023-05-14 | archiveurl = https://web.archive.org/web/20221128013234/https://www.okinawastory.jp/spot/1304 | archivedate = 2022-11-28 }}</ref>）は、[[沖縄県]][[竹富町]]の[[新城島]]（上地島）にある[[御嶽 (沖縄)|御嶽]]。'''磯御嶽'''（いしゃーうがん、いしょーうがん）、'''グスクウガン'''{{Sfn|町史|2013|p=412}}（クースクウガン<ref name="hesiki_1975">{{cite journal|和書 | title = 新城島のアカムタ・クロムタ祭祀 : 上地のウフプール覚書 | author = 平敷令治 | url = http://hdl.handle.net/2308/1372 | journal = 沖縄国際大学文学部紀要 社会学科篇 | issn = 02870681 | publisher = 沖縄国際大学 | year = 1975 | month = 03 | volume = 3 | issue = 1 | pages = 55-69 }}</ref>）、'''ザンヌオン'''<ref name="shojiten">{{cite book|和書 | title = 八重山ジャンルごと小事典 | page = 91 | author = 崎原恒新 | publisher = [[ボーダーインク]] | date = 1999-08-01 }}</ref>とも呼ばれる。人魚神社という通称で呼ばれることもあるが、[[神社]]ではない<ref>{{cite encyclopedia | language = ja | title = 新城島 | encyclopedia= 角川日本地名大辞典 47 沖縄県 | author = 角川日本地名大辞典編纂委員会 | date= 1986-07-08 | publisher= 角川書店 | page = 130 }}</ref>。
== 概要 == 新城島を構成する2島のうち、北側に位置する上地島には4つの御嶽がある。東御嶽はそのうち最も歴史が古く、美御嶽（ナハウガン）次ぐ聖地とされる御嶽である。上地島唯一の集落の東（アール）に位置し、[[龍宮]]の神を祀る{{Sfn|町史|2013|p=412}}<ref name="hesiki_1975" />。御嶽は岩石の台地上にあり、拝所の裏には石垣で囲まれた屋敷跡がある{{Sfn|町史|2013|p=414}}。周辺からは[[パナリ焼き]]が出土する<ref name="shojiten" />。
御嶽に続く道には「立ち入り禁止」の札が掛かっており、氏子以外は鳥居から先に立ち入ることはできない<ref name="okinawatimes_1999-12-18">{{cite news | title = 島を往く・船人くらし(8) 新城島（竹富町） | url = http://www.okinawatimes.co.jp/spe/yuku991218.html | newspaper = 沖縄タイムス | date = 1999-12-18 | archiveurl = https://web.archive.org/web/20000901000807/http://www.okinawatimes.co.jp/spe/yuku991218.html | archivedate = 2000-09-01 }}</ref>。
== 歴史 == 島の元御嶽（ムトゥウガン）とも呼ばれる御嶽で、上地島に人が住むようになった当初に創建されたと伝えられる{{Sfn|町史|2013|p=414}}。上地島の歴史には不詳な点が多いが、[[1477年]]に[[朝鮮]]・[[済州島]]の漂流民3人が[[与那国島]]に漂着し、島伝いに送還された際に新城島の記録を残しており、その頃には住民が定住していたことが分かる{{Sfn|町史|2013|pp=97-101}}。
[[琉球王国|琉球王府]]により[[人頭税]]が課せられた時代には、水が乏しく農作物が育たない新城島には[[ジュゴン]]（ザン、ザヌ）の干肉の貢納が命じられた。この御嶽には捕獲したジュゴンの頭骨が奉納され、ジュゴンを含む豊漁が祈願された{{Sfn|町史|2013|p=415}}<ref name="okinawastory" /><ref name="shojiten" />。かつて御嶽にはジュゴンの頭骨が山積みされていた{{Sfn|町史|2013|p=34}}が、盗まれる等してその数は減少している<ref name="okinawatimes_1999-12-18" />。「ザンヌオン」という別名は「ジュゴンの御嶽」という意味であり、「磯御嶽」の「磯」には「海」という意味もある{{Sfn|町史|2013|p=414}}<ref name="hesiki_1975" />。
== 祭祀 == 島の三大行事である豊年祭（ブル）、節祭（シ<sub>ヰ</sub>チ<sub>ヰ</sub>）、結願祭（キチュガン）で、祈願の対象となる{{Sfn|町史|2013|p=415}}。
神司{{Efn|祭祀を司る女性神職。}}は安里家（アールアサデー）、カマンガー{{Efn|神司を補佐する男性神職。}}は新家（アーラエー）である{{Sfn|町史|2013|p=412}}が、2013年時点で神司は不在である{{Sfn|町史|2013|p=415}}。
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} === 注釈 === {{Notelist}} === 出典 === {{Reflist|2}}
== 参考文献 == * {{cite book|和書 | title = 竹富町史 第五巻 新城島 | editor = 竹富町史編集委員会 | date = 2013-11-30 | ref = {{SfnRef|町史|2013}} }}
== 関連項目 == * [[アカマタ・クロマタ]]

== 概要 == 新城島を構成する2島のうち、北側に位置する上地島には4つの御嶽がある。東御嶽はそのうち最も歴史が古く、美御嶽（ナハウガン）次ぐ聖地とされる御嶽である。上地島唯一の集落の東（アール）に位置し、[[龍宮]]の神を祀る{{Sfn|町史|2013|p=412}}<ref name="hesiki_1975" />。御嶽は岩石の台地上にあり、拝所の裏には石垣で囲まれた屋敷跡がある{{Sfn|町史|2013|p=414}}。周辺からは[[パナリ焼き]]が出土する<ref name="shojiten" />。

御嶽に続く道には「立ち入り禁止」の札が掛かっており、氏子以外は鳥居から先に立ち入ることはできない<ref name="okinawatimes_1999-12-18">{{cite news | title = 島を往く・船人くらし(8) 新城島（竹富町） | url = http://www.okinawatimes.co.jp/spe/yuku991218.html | newspaper = 沖縄タイムス | date = 1999-12-18 | archiveurl = https://web.archive.org/web/20000901000807/http://www.okinawatimes.co.jp/spe/yuku991218.html | archivedate = 2000-09-01 }}</ref>。
== 歴史 == 島の元御嶽（ムトゥウガン）とも呼ばれる御嶽で、上地島に人が住むようになった当初に創建されたと伝えられる{{Sfn|町史|2013|p=414}}。上地島の歴史には不詳な点が多いが、[[1477年]]に[[朝鮮]]・[[済州島]]の漂流民3人が[[与那国島]]に漂着し、島伝いに送還された際に新城島の記録を残しており、その頃には住民が定住していたことが分かる{{Sfn|町史|2013|pp=97-101}}。
[[琉球王国|琉球王府]]により[[人頭税]]が課せられた時代には、水が乏しく農作物が育たない新城島には[[ジュゴン]]（ザン、ザヌ）の干肉の貢納が命じられた。この御嶽には捕獲したジュゴンの頭骨が奉納され、ジュゴンを含む豊漁が祈願された{{Sfn|町史|2013|p=415}}<ref name="okinawastory" /><ref name="shojiten" />。かつて御嶽にはジュゴンの頭骨が山積みされていた{{Sfn|町史|2013|p=34}}が、盗まれる等してその数は減少している<ref name="okinawatimes_1999-12-18" />。「ザンヌオン」という別名は「ジュゴンの御嶽」という意味であり、「磯御嶽」の「磯」には「海」という意味もある{{Sfn|町史|2013|p=414}}<ref name="hesiki_1975" />。
== 祭祀 == 島の三大行事である豊年祭（ブル）、節祭（シ<sub>ヰ</sub>チ<sub>ヰ</sub>）、結願祭（キチュガン）で、祈願の対象となる{{Sfn|町史|2013|p=415}}。
神司{{Efn|祭祀を司る女性神職。}}は安里家（アールアサデー）、カマンガー{{Efn|神司を補佐する男性神職。}}は新家（アーラエー）である{{Sfn|町史|2013|p=412}}が、2013年時点で神司は不在である{{Sfn|町史|2013|p=415}}。
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} === 注釈 === {{Notelist}} === 出典 === {{Reflist|2}}
== 参考文献 == * {{cite book|和書 | title = 竹富町史 第五巻 新城島 | editor = 竹富町史編集委員会 | date = 2013-11-30 | ref = {{SfnRef|町史|2013}} }}
== 関連項目 == * [[アカマタ・クロマタ]]

revision.text:
'''東御嶽'''（あーるうがん、あーりぃうがん<ref name="okinawastory">{{Cite web|和書 | title = 新城島 | url = https://www.okinawastory.jp/spot/1304 | work = おきなわ物語 | publisher = 一般財団法人沖縄観光コンベンションビューロー | accessdate = 2023-05-14 | archiveurl = https://web.archive.org/web/20221128013234/https://www.okinawastory.jp/spot/1304 | archivedate = 2022-11-28 }}</ref>）は、[[沖縄県]][[竹富町]]の[[新城島]]（上地島）にある[[御嶽 (沖縄)|御嶽]]。'''磯御嶽'''（いしゃーうがん、いしょーうがん）、'''グスクウガン'''{{Sfn|町史|2013|p=412}}（クースクウガン<ref name="hesiki_1975">{{cite journal|和書 | title = 新城島のアカムタ・クロムタ祭祀 : 上地のウフプール覚書 | author = 平敷令治 | url = https://hdl.handle.net/2308/1372 | journal = 沖縄国際大学文学部紀要 社会学科篇 | issn = 02870681 | publisher = 沖縄国際大学 | year = 1975 | month = 03 | volume = 3 | issue = 1 | pages = 55-69 }}</ref>）、'''ザンヌオン'''<ref name="shojiten">{{cite book|和書 | title = 八重山ジャンルごと小事典 | page = 91 | author = 崎原恒新 | publisher = [[ボーダーインク]] | date = 1999-08-01 }}</ref>とも呼ばれる。'''人魚神社'''（にんぎょじんじゃ）という通称で呼ばれることもあるが、[[神社]]ではない<ref>{{cite encyclopedia | language = ja | title = 新城島 | encyclopedia= 角川日本地名大辞典 47 沖縄県 | author = 角川日本地名大辞典編纂委員会 | date= 1986-07-08 | publisher= 角川書店 | page = 130 }}</ref>。
== 概要 == 新城島を構成する2島のうち、北側に位置する上地島には4つの御嶽がある。東御嶽はそのうち最も歴史が古く、美御嶽（ナハウガン）に次ぐ聖地とされる御嶽である。上地島唯一の集落の東（アール）に位置し、[[龍宮]]の神を祀る{{Sfn|町史|2013|p=412}}<ref name="hesiki_1975" />。御嶽は岩石の台地上にあり、拝所の裏には石垣で囲まれた屋敷跡がある{{Sfn|町史|2013|p=414}}。周辺からは[[パナリ焼き]]が出土する<ref name="shojiten" />。
御嶽に続く道には「立ち入り禁止」の札が掛かっており、氏子以外は鳥居から先に立ち入ることはできない<ref name="okinawatimes_1999-12-18">{{cite news | title = 島を往く・船人くらし(8) 新城島（竹富町） | url = http://www.okinawatimes.co.jp/spe/yuku991218.html | newspaper = 沖縄タイムス | date = 1999-12-18 | archiveurl = https://web.archive.org/web/20000901000807/http://www.okinawatimes.co.jp/spe/yuku991218.html | archivedate = 2000-09-01 }}</ref>。
== 歴史 == 島の元御嶽（ムトゥウガン）とも呼ばれる御嶽で、上地島に人が住むようになった当初に創建されたと伝えられる{{Sfn|町史|2013|p=414}}。上地島の歴史には不詳な点が多いが、[[1477年]]に[[朝鮮]]・[[済州島]]の漂流民3人が[[与那国島]]に漂着し、島伝いに送還された際に新城島の記録を残しており、その頃には住民が定住していたことが分かる{{Sfn|町史|2013|pp=97-101}}。
[[琉球王国|琉球王府]]により[[人頭税]]が課せられた時代には、水が乏しく農作物が育たない新城島には[[ジュゴン]]（ザン、ザヌ）の干肉の貢納が命じられた。この御嶽には捕獲したジュゴンの頭骨が奉納され、ジュゴンを含む豊漁が祈願された{{Sfn|町史|2013|p=415}}<ref name="okinawastory" /><ref name="shojiten" />。かつて御嶽にはジュゴンの頭骨が山積みされていた{{Sfn|町史|2013|p=34}}が、盗まれる等してその数は減少している<ref name="okinawatimes_1999-12-18" />。「ザンヌオン」という別名は「ジュゴンの御嶽」という意味であり、「磯御嶽」の「磯」には「海」という意味もある{{Sfn|町史|2013|p=414}}<ref name="hesiki_1975" />。
== 祭祀 == 島の三大行事である豊年祭（ブル）、節祭（シ<sub>ヰ</sub>チ<sub>ヰ</sub>）、結願祭（キチュガン）で、祈願の対象となる{{Sfn|町史|2013|p=415}}。
神司{{Efn|祭祀を司る女性神職。}}は安里家（アールアサデー）、カマンガー{{Efn|神司を補佐する男性神職。}}は新家（アーラエー）である{{Sfn|町史|2013|p=412}}が、2013年時点で神司は不在である{{Sfn|町史|2013|p=415}}。
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} === 注釈 === {{Notelist}} === 出典 === {{Reflist|2}}
== 参考文献 == * {{cite book|和書 | title = 竹富町史 第五巻 新城島 | editor = 竹富町史編集委員会 | date = 2013-11-30 | ref = {{SfnRef|町史|2013}} }}
== 関連項目 == * [[アカマタ・クロマタ]]

== 関連項目 == * [[アカマタ・クロマタ]]
{{Normdaten}} {{Reli-stub}} {{Pref-stub|pref=沖縄県}} {{DEFAULTSORT:にんきよしんしや}} [[Category:御嶽 (沖縄)]] [[Category:竹富町の建築物]] [[Category:竹富町の歴史]] [[Category:Utaki (Okinawa)]]

'''東御嶽'''（あーるうがん、あーりぃうがん<ref name="okinawastory">{{Cite web|和書 | title = 新城島 | url = https://www.okinawastory.jp/spot/1304 | work = おきなわ物語 | publisher = 一般財団法人沖縄観光コンベンションビューロー | accessdate = 2023-05-14 | archiveurl = https://web.archive.org/web/20221128013234/https://www.okinawastory.jp/spot/1304 | archivedate = 2022-11-28 }}</ref>）は、[[沖縄県]][[竹富町]]の[[新城島]]（上地島）にある[[御嶽 (沖縄)|御嶽]]。'''磯御嶽'''（いしゃーうがん、いしょーうがん）、'''グスクウガン'''{{Sfn|町史|2013|p=412}}（クースクウガン<ref name="hesiki_1975">{{cite journal|和書 | title = 新城島のアカムタ・クロムタ祭祀 : 上地のウフプール覚書 | author = 平敷令治 | url = https://hdl.handle.net/2308/1372 | journal = 沖縄国際大学文学部紀要 社会学科篇 | issn = 02870681 | publisher = 沖縄国際大学 | year = 1975 | month = 03 | volume = 3 | issue = 1 | pages = 55-69 }}</ref>）、'''ザンヌオン'''<ref name="shojiten">{{cite book|和書 | title = 八重山ジャンルごと小事典 | page = 91 | author = 崎原恒新 | publisher = [[ボーダーインク]] | date = 1999-08-01 }}</ref>とも呼ばれる。'''人魚神社'''（にんぎょじんじゃ）という通称で呼ばれることもあるが、[[神社]]ではない<ref>{{cite encyclopedia | language = ja | title = 新城島 | encyclopedia= 角川日本地名大辞典 47 沖縄県 | author = 角川日本地名大辞典編纂委員会 | date= 1986-07-08 | publisher= 角川書店 | page = 130 }}</ref>。
== 概要 == 新城島を構成する2島のうち、北側に位置する上地島には4つの御嶽がある。東御嶽はそのうち最も歴史が古く、美御嶽（ナハウガン）に次ぐ聖地とされる御嶽である。上地島唯一の集落の東（アール）に位置し、[[龍宮]]の神を祀る{{Sfn|町史|2013|p=412}}<ref name="hesiki_1975" />。御嶽は岩石の台地上にあり、拝所の裏には石垣で囲まれた屋敷跡がある{{Sfn|町史|2013|p=414}}。周辺からは[[パナリ焼き]]が出土する<ref name="shojiten" />。
御嶽に続く道には「立ち入り禁止」の札が掛かっており、氏子以外は鳥居から先に立ち入ることはできない<ref name="okinawatimes_1999-12-18">{{cite news | title = 島を往く・船人くらし(8) 新城島（竹富町） | url = http://www.okinawatimes.co.jp/spe/yuku991218.html | newspaper = 沖縄タイムス | date = 1999-12-18 | archiveurl = https://web.archive.org/web/20000901000807/http://www.okinawatimes.co.jp/spe/yuku991218.html | archivedate = 2000-09-01 }}</ref>。
== 歴史 == 島の元御嶽（ムトゥウガン）とも呼ばれる御嶽で、上地島に人が住むようになった当初に創建されたと伝えられる{{Sfn|町史|2013|p=414}}。上地島の歴史には不詳な点が多いが、[[1477年]]に[[朝鮮]]・[[済州島]]の漂流民3人が[[与那国島]]に漂着し、島伝いに送還された際に新城島の記録を残しており、その頃には住民が定住していたことが分かる{{Sfn|町史|2013|pp=97-101}}。
[[琉球王国|琉球王府]]により[[人頭税]]が課せられた時代には、水が乏しく農作物が育たない新城島には[[ジュゴン]]（ザン、ザヌ）の干肉の貢納が命じられた。この御嶽には捕獲したジュゴンの頭骨が奉納され、ジュゴンを含む豊漁が祈願された{{Sfn|町史|2013|p=415}}<ref name="okinawastory" /><ref name="shojiten" />。かつて御嶽にはジュゴンの頭骨が山積みされていた{{Sfn|町史|2013|p=34}}が、盗まれる等してその数は減少している<ref name="okinawatimes_1999-12-18" />。「ザンヌオン」という別名は「ジュゴンの御嶽」という意味であり、「磯御嶽」の「磯」には「海」という意味もある{{Sfn|町史|2013|p=414}}<ref name="hesiki_1975" />。
== 祭祀 == 島の三大行事である豊年祭（ブル）、節祭（シ<sub>ヰ</sub>チ<sub>ヰ</sub>）、結願祭（キチュガン）で、祈願の対象となる{{Sfn|町史|2013|p=415}}。
神司{{Efn|祭祀を司る女性神職。}}は安里家（アールアサデー）、カマンガー{{Efn|神司を補佐する男性神職。}}は新家（アーラエー）である{{Sfn|町史|2013|p=412}}が、2013年時点で神司は不在である{{Sfn|町史|2013|p=415}}。
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} === 注釈 === {{Notelist}} === 出典 === {{Reflist|2}}
== 参考文献 == * {{cite book|和書 | title = 竹富町史 第五巻 新城島 | editor = 竹富町史編集委員会 | date = 2013-11-30 | ref = {{SfnRef|町史|2013}} }}
== 関連項目 == * [[アカマタ・クロマタ]]

== 概要 == 新城島を構成する2島のうち、北側に位置する上地島には4つの御嶽がある。東御嶽はそのうち最も歴史が古く、美御嶽（ナハウガン）に次ぐ聖地とされる御嶽である。上地島唯一の集落の東（アール）に位置し、[[龍宮]]の神を祀る{{Sfn|町史|2013|p=412}}<ref name="hesiki_1975" />。御嶽は岩石の台地上にあり、拝所の裏には石垣で囲まれた屋敷跡がある{{Sfn|町史|2013|p=414}}。周辺からは[[パナリ焼き]]が出土する<ref name="shojiten" />。
御嶽に続く道には「立ち入り禁止」の札が掛かっており、氏子以外は鳥居から先に立ち入ることはできない<ref name="okinawatimes_1999-12-18">{{cite news | title = 島を往く・船人くらし(8) 新城島（竹富町） | url = http://www.okinawatimes.co.jp/spe/yuku991218.html | newspaper = 沖縄タイムス | date = 1999-12-18 | archiveurl = https://web.archive.org/web/20000901000807/http://www.okinawatimes.co.jp/spe/yuku991218.html | archivedate = 2000-09-01 }}</ref>。
== 歴史 == 島の元御嶽（ムトゥウガン）とも呼ばれる御嶽で、上地島に人が住むようになった当初に創建されたと伝えられる{{Sfn|町史|2013|p=414}}。上地島の歴史には不詳な点が多いが、[[1477年]]に[[朝鮮]]・[[済州島]]の漂流民3人が[[与那国島]]に漂着し、島伝いに送還された際に新城島の記録を残しており、その頃には住民が定住していたことが分かる{{Sfn|町史|2013|pp=97-101}}。
[[琉球王国|琉球王府]]により[[人頭税]]が課せられた時代には、水が乏しく農作物が育たない新城島には[[ジュゴン]]（ザン、ザヌ）の干肉の貢納が命じられた。この御嶽には捕獲したジュゴンの頭骨が奉納され、ジュゴンを含む豊漁が祈願された{{Sfn|町史|2013|p=415}}<ref name="okinawastory" /><ref name="shojiten" />。かつて御嶽にはジュゴンの頭骨が山積みされていた{{Sfn|町史|2013|p=34}}が、盗まれる等してその数は減少している<ref name="okinawatimes_1999-12-18" />。「ザンヌオン」という別名は「ジュゴンの御嶽」という意味であり、「磯御嶽」の「磯」には「海」という意味もある{{Sfn|町史|2013|p=414}}<ref name="hesiki_1975" />。
== 祭祀 == 島の三大行事である豊年祭（ブル）、節祭（シ<sub>ヰ</sub>チ<sub>ヰ</sub>）、結願祭（キチュガン）で、祈願の対象となる{{Sfn|町史|2013|p=415}}。
神司{{Efn|祭祀を司る女性神職。}}は安里家（アールアサデー）、カマンガー{{Efn|神司を補佐する男性神職。}}は新家（アーラエー）である{{Sfn|町史|2013|p=412}}が、2013年時点で神司は不在である{{Sfn|町史|2013|p=415}}。
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} === 注釈 === {{Notelist}} === 出典 === {{Reflist|2}}
== 参考文献 == * {{cite book|和書 | title = 竹富町史 第五巻 新城島 | editor = 竹富町史編集委員会 | date = 2013-11-30 | ref = {{SfnRef|町史|2013}} }}
== 関連項目 == * [[アカマタ・クロマタ]]

revision.text:
'''東御嶽'''（あーるうがん、あーりぃうがん<ref name="okinawastory">{{Cite web|和書 | title = 新城島 | url = https://www.okinawastory.jp/spot/1304 | work = おきなわ物語 | publisher = 一般財団法人沖縄観光コンベンションビューロー | accessdate = 2023-05-14 | archiveurl = https://web.archive.org/web/20221128013234/https://www.okinawastory.jp/spot/1304 | archivedate = 2022-11-28 }}</ref>）は、[[沖縄県]][[竹富町]]の[[新城島]]（上地島）にある[[御嶽 (沖縄)|御嶽]]。'''磯御嶽'''（いしゃーうがん、いしょーうがん）、'''グスクウガン'''{{Sfn|町史|2013|p=412}}（クースクウガン<ref name="hesiki_1975">{{cite journal|和書 | title = 新城島のアカムタ・クロムタ祭祀 : 上地のウフプール覚書 | author = 平敷令治 | url = https://hdl.handle.net/2308/1372 | journal = 沖縄国際大学文学部紀要 社会学科篇 | issn = 02870681 | publisher = 沖縄国際大学 | year = 1975 | month = 03 | volume = 3 | issue = 1 | pages = 55-69 }}</ref>）、'''ザンヌオン'''<ref name="shojiten">{{cite book|和書 | title = 八重山ジャンルごと小事典 | page = 91 | author = 崎原恒新 | publisher = [[ボーダーインク]] | date = 1999-08-01 }}</ref>とも呼ばれる。'''人魚神社'''（にんぎょじんじゃ）という通称で呼ばれることもあるが、[[神社]]ではない<ref>{{cite encyclopedia | language = ja | title = 新城島 | encyclopedia= 角川日本地名大辞典 47 沖縄県 | author = 角川日本地名大辞典編纂委員会 | date= 1986-07-08 | publisher= 角川書店 | page = 130 }}</ref>。
== 概要 == 新城島を構成する2島のうち、北側に位置する上地島には4つの御嶽がある。東御嶽はそのうち最も歴史が古く、美御嶽（ナハウガン）に次ぐ聖地とされる御嶽である。上地島唯一の集落の東（アール）に位置し、[[龍宮]]の神を祀る{{Sfn|町史|2013|p=412}}<ref name="hesiki_1975" />。御嶽は岩石の台地上にあり、拝所の裏には石垣で囲まれた屋敷跡がある{{Sfn|町史|2013|p=414}}。周辺からは[[パナリ焼き]]が出土する<ref name="shojiten" />。
御嶽に続く道には「立ち入り禁止」の札が掛かっており、氏子以外は鳥居から先に立ち入ることはできない<ref name="okinawatimes_1999-12-18">{{cite news | title = 島を往く・船人くらし(8) 新城島（竹富町） | url = http://www.okinawatimes.co.jp/spe/yuku991218.html | newspaper = 沖縄タイムス | date = 1999-12-18 | archiveurl = https://web.archive.org/web/20000901000807/http://www.okinawatimes.co.jp/spe/yuku991218.html | archivedate = 2000-09-01 }}</ref>。
== 歴史 == 島の元御嶽（ムトゥウガン）とも呼ばれる御嶽で、上地島に人が住むようになった当初に創建されたと伝えられる{{Sfn|町史|2013|p=414}}。上地島の歴史には不詳な点が多いが、[[1477年]]に[[朝鮮]]・[[済州島]]の漂流民3人が[[与那国島]]に漂着し、島伝いに送還された際に新城島の記録を残しており、その頃には住民が定住していたことが分かる{{Sfn|町史|2013|pp=97-101}}。
[[琉球王国|琉球王府]]により[[人頭税]]が課せられた時代には、水が乏しく農作物が育たない新城島には[[ジュゴン]]（ザン、ザヌ）の干肉の貢納が命じられた。この御嶽には捕獲したジュゴンの頭骨が奉納され、ジュゴンを含む豊漁が祈願された{{Sfn|町史|2013|p=415}}<ref name="okinawastory" /><ref name="shojiten" />。かつて御嶽にはジュゴンの頭骨が山積みされていた{{Sfn|町史|2013|p=34}}が、盗まれる等してその数は減少している<ref name="okinawatimes_1999-12-18" />。「ザンヌオン」という別名は「ジュゴンの御嶽」という意味であり、「磯御嶽」の「磯」には「海」という意味もある{{Sfn|町史|2013|p=414}}<ref name="hesiki_1975" />。
== 祭祀 == 島の三大行事である豊年祭（ブル）、節祭（シ<sub>ヰ</sub>チ<sub>ヰ</sub>）、結願祭（キチュガン）で、祈願の対象となる{{Sfn|町史|2013|p=415}}。
神司{{Efn|祭祀を司る女性神職。}}は安里家（アールアサデー）、カマンガー{{Efn|神司を補佐する男性神職。}}は新家（アーラエー）である{{Sfn|町史|2013|p=412}}が、2013年時点で神司は不在である{{Sfn|町史|2013|p=415}}。

== 参考文献 == * {{cite book|和書 | title = 竹富町史 第五巻 新城島 | editor = 竹富町史編集委員会 | date = 2013-11-30 | ref = {{SfnRef|町史|2013}} }}

御嶽に続く道には「立ち入り禁止」の札が掛かっており、氏子以外は鳥居から先に立ち入ることはできない<ref name="okinawatimes_1999-12-18">{{cite news | title = 島を往く・船人くらし(8) 新城島（竹富町） | url = http://www.okinawatimes.co.jp/spe/yuku991218.html | newspaper = 沖縄タイムス | date = 1999-12-18 | archiveurl = https://web.archive.org/web/20000901000807/http://www.okinawatimes.co.jp/spe/yuku991218.html | archivedate = 2000-09-01 }}</ref>。
== 歴史 == 島の元御嶽（ムトゥウガン）とも呼ばれる御嶽で、上地島に人が住むようになった当初に創建されたと伝えられる{{Sfn|町史|2013|p=414}}。上地島の歴史には不詳な点が多いが、[[1477年]]に[[朝鮮]]・[[済州島]]の漂流民3人が[[与那国島]]に漂着し、島伝いに送還された際に新城島の記録を残しており、その頃には住民が定住していたことが分かる{{Sfn|町史|2013|pp=97-101}}。
[[琉球王国|琉球王府]]により[[人頭税]]が課せられた時代には、水が乏しく農作物が育たない新城島には[[ジュゴン]]（ザン、ザヌ）の干肉の貢納が命じられた。この御嶽には捕獲したジュゴンの頭骨が奉納され、ジュゴンを含む豊漁が祈願された{{Sfn|町史|2013|p=415}}<ref name="okinawastory" /><ref name="shojiten" />。かつて御嶽にはジュゴンの頭骨が山積みされていた{{Sfn|町史|2013|p=34}}が、盗まれる等してその数は減少している<ref name="okinawatimes_1999-12-18" />。「ザンヌオン」という別名は「ジュゴンの御嶽」という意味であり、「磯御嶽」の「磯」には「海」という意味もある{{Sfn|町史|2013|p=414}}<ref name="hesiki_1975" />。
== 祭祀 == 島の三大行事である豊年祭（ブル）、節祭（シ<sub>ヰ</sub>チ<sub>ヰ</sub>）、結願祭（キチュガン）で、祈願の対象となる{{Sfn|町史|2013|p=415}}。
神司{{Efn|祭祀を司る女性神職。}}は安里家（アールアサデー）、カマンガー{{Efn|神司を補佐する男性神職。}}は新家（アーラエー）である{{Sfn|町史|2013|p=412}}が、2013年時点で神司は不在である{{Sfn|町史|2013|p=415}}。
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} === 注釈 === {{Notelist}} === 出典 === {{Reflist|2}}
== 参考文献 == * {{cite book|和書 | title = 竹富町史 第五巻 新城島 | editor = 竹富町史編集委員会 | date = 2013-11-30 | ref = {{SfnRef|町史|2013}} }}
== 関連項目 == * [[アカマタ・クロマタ]]

revision.text:
{{infobox religious building |name=Ārīugan }} '''Higashi Utaki''' (東御嶽, Āruugan, Ārīugan<ref name="okinawastory">{{Cite web|ja | title = Aragusuku Island | url = https://www.okinawastory.jp/spot/1304 | work = Okinawa Story | publisher = Okinawa Convention & Visitors Bureau | accessdate = 2023-05-14 | archiveurl = https://web.archive.org/web/20221128013234/https://www.okinawastory.jp/spot/1304 | archivedate = 2022-11-28}}</ref>) is an {{ill|Utaki (Okinawa)|lt=Utaki|ja|御嶽|1=Utaki|2=ja|3=御嶽 (沖縄)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} located on {{ill|Aragusuku Island|ja|新城島|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Kamiji Island), in {{ill|Taketomi, Okinawa|ja|竹富町|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Okinawa Prefecture|ja|沖縄県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. It is also known as '''Iso Utaki''' (磯御嶽, Ishāugan, Ishōugan), '''Gusuku Utaki''' (グスクウガン, Kūsukuugan<ref name="hesiki_1975">https://hdl.handle.net/2308/1372 </ref>), and '''Zannuon'''<ref name="shojiten">{{cite book|ja | title = Yaeyama Small Encyclopedia | page = 91 | author = Tsuneshin Sakihara | publisher = Border Ink | date = 1999-08-01}}</ref>. It is sometimes colloquially referred to as the "Mermaid Shrine" (人魚神社, Ningyo Jinja), though it is not a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}<ref>{{cite encyclopedia|ja | title = Aragusuku Island | encyclopedia= Kadokawa Japan Place Name Encyclopedia 47 Okinawa Prefecture | author = Kadokawa Japan Place Name Compilation Committee | date= 1986-07-08 | publisher= Kadokawa Shoten | page = 130}}</ref>.
== Overview == Of the four Utaki on Kamiji Island, Higashi Utaki is the oldest and is regarded as the second holiest site after {{ill|Bi Utaki|ja|美御嶽|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Naha-ugan). It is located east (Āru) of the only settlement on Kamiji Island and enshrines the deity of {{ill|Ryūgū|ja|龍宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}}<ref name="hesiki_1975" /><ref name="okinawastory" />. The site is situated on a rocky plateau, and behind the worship area is the remains of an enclosure surrounded by stone walls<ref name="shojiten" />. Fragments of {{ill|Panari pottery|ja|パナリ焼き|qq=draft|qq=draft}} have been found around the site<ref name="shojiten" />.
Access to the Utaki is restricted, with a "No Entry" sign posted on the path leading to it. Only designated shrine caretakers are permitted to enter beyond the torii gate<ref name="okinawatimes_1999-12-18">{{cite news|ja | title = Island Life: Seafarers' Living (8) Aragusuku Island (Taketomi Town) | url = http://www.okinawatimes.co.jp/spe/yuku991218.html | newspaper = Okinawa Times | date = 1999-12-18 | archiveurl = https://web.archive.org/web/20000901000807/http://www.okinawatimes.co.jp/spe/yuku991218.html | archivedate = 2000-09-01}}</ref>.
== History == Higashi Utaki is also referred to as the "Original Utaki" (Mutuuugan), as it is believed to have been established when people first settled on Kamiji Island<ref name="hesiki_1975" />. Though the history of the island remains unclear, records from [[1477]] indicate that three shipwrecked Koreans from [[Jeju Island]] were rescued and repatriated through Aragusuku, confirming that the island had a settled population at that time. During the era when the {{ill|Ryukyu Kingdom|ja|琉球王国|qq=draft}} imposed the {{ill|Head Tax|ja|人頭税|qq=draft}}, Aragusuku Island, which had limited fresh water and farmland, was required to provide dried {{ill|Dugong|ja|ジュゴン|qq=draft}} meat as tribute. The skulls of captured dugongs were offered at Higashi Utaki as part of prayers for abundant marine harvests. At one time, a large number of dugong skulls were piled at the site, but theft and other factors have significantly reduced their number. The name "Zannuon" means "Dugong Utaki," and "Iso" in "Iso Utaki" can also mean "sea".
== Rituals == Higashi Utaki is a focal point for prayers during the three major festivals of the island: the Harvest Festival (Buru), the Seasonal Festival (Siichi), and the Kichugan Festival (Kichugan). The {{ill|Kaminchu|ja|神司|qq=draft}} (priestess) responsible for the shrine traditionally comes from the Asato family (Āru Asadei), while the {{ill|Kamanga|ja|カマンガー|qq=draft}} (assistant male priest) comes from the Shin family (Āraei). As of 2013, the shrine has no serving priestess. == References ==
== Bibliography ==
* {{cite book|ja|title=Taketomi Town History Volume 5: Aragusuku Island|editor=Taketomi Town History Compilation Committee|date=2013-11-30|ref={{SfnRef|町史|2013}}}}
== See also ==
* {{ill|Akamata and Kuromata|ja|アカマタ・クロマタ|qq=draft}} {{coord|24|18|15.07|N|123|53|49.68|E|type:landmark_region:JP-47|display=title}} {{Normdaten}} {{Reli-stub}} {{Pref-stub|pref=沖縄県}} {{citation needed|にんきよしんしや}}{{Okinawan Shrines}}{{DEFAULTSORT:A}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Ārīugan}} {{translated page|ja|東御嶽 (竹富町新城上地)|version=98122603|comment=Imported full ja history}}

== Rituals == Higashi Utaki is a focal point for prayers during the three major festivals of the island: the Harvest Festival (Buru), the Seasonal Festival (Siichi), and the Kichugan Festival (Kichugan). The {{ill|Kaminchu|ja|神司|qq=draft}} (priestess) responsible for the shrine traditionally comes from the Asato family (Āru Asadei), while the {{ill|Kamanga|ja|カマンガー|qq=draft}} (assistant male priest) comes from the Shin family (Āraei). As of 2013, the shrine has no serving priestess. == References ==

[[ja:東御嶽 (竹富町新城上地)]] [[sn:Ārīugan]] [[ja:Ārīugan]]

{{infobox religious building |name=Ārīugan }} '''Higashi Utaki''' (東御嶽, Āruugan, Ārīugan<ref name="okinawastory">{{Cite web|ja | title = Aragusuku Island | url = https://www.okinawastory.jp/spot/1304 | work = Okinawa Story | publisher = Okinawa Convention & Visitors Bureau | accessdate = 2023-05-14 | archiveurl = https://web.archive.org/web/20221128013234/https://www.okinawastory.jp/spot/1304 | archivedate = 2022-11-28}}</ref>) is an {{ill|Utaki (Okinawa)|lt=Utaki|ja|御嶽|1=Utaki|2=ja|3=御嶽 (沖縄)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} located on {{ill|Aragusuku Island|ja|新城島|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Kamiji Island), in {{ill|Taketomi, Okinawa|ja|竹富町|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Okinawa Prefecture|ja|沖縄県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. It is also known as '''Iso Utaki''' (磯御嶽, Ishāugan, Ishōugan), '''Gusuku Utaki''' (グスクウガン, Kūsukuugan<ref name="hesiki_1975">https://hdl.handle.net/2308/1372 </ref>), and '''Zannuon'''<ref name="shojiten">{{cite book|ja | title = Yaeyama Small Encyclopedia | page = 91 | author = Tsuneshin Sakihara | publisher = Border Ink | date = 1999-08-01}}</ref>. It is sometimes colloquially referred to as the "Mermaid Shrine" (人魚神社, Ningyo Jinja), though it is not a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}<ref>{{cite encyclopedia|ja | title = Aragusuku Island | encyclopedia= Kadokawa Japan Place Name Encyclopedia 47 Okinawa Prefecture | author = Kadokawa Japan Place Name Compilation Committee | date= 1986-07-08 | publisher= Kadokawa Shoten | page = 130}}</ref>.
== Overview == Of the four Utaki on Kamiji Island, Higashi Utaki is the oldest and is regarded as the second holiest site after {{ill|Bi Utaki|ja|美御嶽|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Naha-ugan). It is located east (Āru) of the only settlement on Kamiji Island and enshrines the deity of {{ill|Ryūgū|ja|龍宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}}<ref name="hesiki_1975" /><ref name="okinawastory" />. The site is situated on a rocky plateau, and behind the worship area is the remains of an enclosure surrounded by stone walls<ref name="shojiten" />. Fragments of {{ill|Panari pottery|ja|パナリ焼き|qq=draft|qq=draft}} have been found around the site<ref name="shojiten" />.
Access to the Utaki is restricted, with a "No Entry" sign posted on the path leading to it. Only designated shrine caretakers are permitted to enter beyond the torii gate<ref name="okinawatimes_1999-12-18">{{cite news|ja | title = Island Life: Seafarers' Living (8) Aragusuku Island (Taketomi Town) | url = http://www.okinawatimes.co.jp/spe/yuku991218.html | newspaper = Okinawa Times | date = 1999-12-18 | archiveurl = https://web.archive.org/web/20000901000807/http://www.okinawatimes.co.jp/spe/yuku991218.html | archivedate = 2000-09-01}}</ref>.
== History == Higashi Utaki is also referred to as the "Original Utaki" (Mutuuugan), as it is believed to have been established when people first settled on Kamiji Island<ref name="hesiki_1975" />. Though the history of the island remains unclear, records from [[1477]] indicate that three shipwrecked Koreans from [[Jeju Island]] were rescued and repatriated through Aragusuku, confirming that the island had a settled population at that time. During the era when the {{ill|Ryukyu Kingdom|ja|琉球王国|qq=draft}} imposed the {{ill|Head Tax|ja|人頭税|qq=draft}}, Aragusuku Island, which had limited fresh water and farmland, was required to provide dried {{ill|Dugong|ja|ジュゴン|qq=draft}} meat as tribute. The skulls of captured dugongs were offered at Higashi Utaki as part of prayers for abundant marine harvests. At one time, a large number of dugong skulls were piled at the site, but theft and other factors have significantly reduced their number. The name "Zannuon" means "Dugong Utaki," and "Iso" in "Iso Utaki" can also mean "sea".
== Rituals == Higashi Utaki is a focal point for prayers during the three major festivals of the island: the Harvest Festival (Buru), the Seasonal Festival (Siichi), and the Kichugan Festival (Kichugan). The {{ill|Kaminchu|ja|神司|qq=draft}} (priestess) responsible for the shrine traditionally comes from the Asato family (Āru Asadei), while the {{ill|Kamanga|ja|カマンガー|qq=draft}} (assistant male priest) comes from the Shin family (Āraei). As of 2013, the shrine has no serving priestess. == References ==

{{infobox religious building |name=Ārīugan }} '''Higashi Utaki''' (東御嶽, Āruugan, Ārīugan<ref name="okinawastory">{{Cite web|ja | title = Aragusuku Island | url = https://www.okinawastory.jp/spot/1304 | work = Okinawa Story | publisher = Okinawa Convention & Visitors Bureau | accessdate = 2023-05-14 | archiveurl = https://web.archive.org/web/20221128013234/https://www.okinawastory.jp/spot/1304 | archivedate = 2022-11-28}}</ref>) is an {{ill|Utaki (Okinawa)|lt=Utaki|ja|御嶽|1=Utaki|2=ja|3=御嶽 (沖縄)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} located on {{ill|Aragusuku Island|ja|新城島|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Kamiji Island), in {{ill|Taketomi, Okinawa|ja|竹富町|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Okinawa Prefecture|ja|沖縄県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. It is also known as '''Iso Utaki''' (磯御嶽, Ishāugan, Ishōugan), '''Gusuku Utaki''' (グスクウガン, Kūsukuugan<ref name="hesiki_1975">https://hdl.handle.net/2308/1372 </ref>), and '''Zannuon'''<ref name="shojiten">{{cite book|ja | title = Yaeyama Small Encyclopedia | page = 91 | author = Tsuneshin Sakihara | publisher = Border Ink | date = 1999-08-01}}</ref>. It is sometimes colloquially referred to as the "Mermaid Shrine" (人魚神社, Ningyo Jinja), though it is not a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}<ref>{{cite encyclopedia|ja | title = Aragusuku Island | encyclopedia= Kadokawa Japan Place Name Encyclopedia 47 Okinawa Prefecture | author = Kadokawa Japan Place Name Compilation Committee | date= 1986-07-08 | publisher= Kadokawa Shoten | page = 130}}</ref>.
== Overview == Of the four Utaki on Kamiji Island, Higashi Utaki is the oldest and is regarded as the second holiest site after {{ill|Bi Utaki|ja|美御嶽|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Naha-ugan). It is located east (Āru) of the only settlement on Kamiji Island and enshrines the deity of {{ill|Ryūgū|ja|龍宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}}<ref name="hesiki_1975" /><ref name="okinawastory" />. The site is situated on a rocky plateau, and behind the worship area is the remains of an enclosure surrounded by stone walls<ref name="shojiten" />. Fragments of {{ill|Panari pottery|ja|パナリ焼き|qq=draft|qq=draft}} have been found around the site<ref name="shojiten" />.
Access to the Utaki is restricted, with a "No Entry" sign posted on the path leading to it. Only designated shrine caretakers are permitted to enter beyond the torii gate<ref name="okinawatimes_1999-12-18">{{cite news|ja | title = Island Life: Seafarers' Living (8) Aragusuku Island (Taketomi Town) | url = http://www.okinawatimes.co.jp/spe/yuku991218.html | newspaper = Okinawa Times | date = 1999-12-18 | archiveurl = https://web.archive.org/web/20000901000807/http://www.okinawatimes.co.jp/spe/yuku991218.html | archivedate = 2000-09-01}}</ref>.
== History == Higashi Utaki is also referred to as the "Original Utaki" (Mutuuugan), as it is believed to have been established when people first settled on Kamiji Island<ref name="hesiki_1975" />. Though the history of the island remains unclear, records from [[1477]] indicate that three shipwrecked Koreans from [[Jeju Island]] were rescued and repatriated through Aragusuku, confirming that the island had a settled population at that time. During the era when the {{ill|Ryukyu Kingdom|ja|琉球王国|qq=draft}} imposed the {{ill|Head Tax|ja|人頭税|qq=draft}}, Aragusuku Island, which had limited fresh water and farmland, was required to provide dried {{ill|Dugong|ja|ジュゴン|qq=draft}} meat as tribute. The skulls of captured dugongs were offered at Higashi Utaki as part of prayers for abundant marine harvests. At one time, a large number of dugong skulls were piled at the site, but theft and other factors have significantly reduced their number. The name "Zannuon" means "Dugong Utaki," and "Iso" in "Iso Utaki" can also mean "sea".
== Rituals == Higashi Utaki is a focal point for prayers during the three major festivals of the island: the Harvest Festival (Buru), the Seasonal Festival (Siichi), and the Kichugan Festival (Kichugan). The {{ill|Kaminchu|ja|神司|qq=draft}} (priestess) responsible for the shrine traditionally comes from the Asato family (Āru Asadei), while the {{ill|Kamanga|ja|カマンガー|qq=draft}} (assistant male priest) comes from the Shin family (Āraei). As of 2013, the shrine has no serving priestess. == References ==
== Bibliography ==
* {{cite book|ja|title=Taketomi Town History Volume 5: Aragusuku Island|editor=Taketomi Town History Compilation Committee|date=2013-11-30|ref={{SfnRef|町史|2013}}}}

{{infobox religious building |name=Ārīugan }} '''Higashi Utaki''' (東御嶽, Āruugan, Ārīugan<ref name="okinawastory">{{Cite web|ja | title = Aragusuku Island | url = https://www.okinawastory.jp/spot/1304 | work = Okinawa Story | publisher = Okinawa Convention & Visitors Bureau | accessdate = 2023-05-14 | archiveurl = https://web.archive.org/web/20221128013234/https://www.okinawastory.jp/spot/1304 | archivedate = 2022-11-28}}</ref>) is an [[:en:Ontake|Utaki]] located on [[:en:Aragusuku Islands|Aragusuku Island]] (Kamiji Island), in [[:en:Taketomi, Okinawa|Taketomi, Okinawa]], [[:en:Okinawa Prefecture|Okinawa Prefecture]]. It is also known as '''Iso Utaki''' (磯御嶽, Ishāugan, Ishōugan), '''Gusuku Utaki''' (グスクウガン, Kūsukuugan<ref name="hesiki_1975">https://hdl.handle.net/2308/1372 </ref>), and '''Zannuon'''<ref name="shojiten">{{cite book|ja | title = Yaeyama Small Encyclopedia | page = 91 | author = Tsuneshin Sakihara | publisher = Border Ink | date = 1999-08-01}}</ref>. It is sometimes colloquially referred to as the "Mermaid Shrine" (人魚神社, Ningyo Jinja), though it is not a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]]<ref>{{cite encyclopedia|ja | title = Aragusuku Island | encyclopedia= Kadokawa Japan Place Name Encyclopedia 47 Okinawa Prefecture | author = Kadokawa Japan Place Name Compilation Committee | date= 1986-07-08 | publisher= Kadokawa Shoten | page = 130}}</ref>.
== Overview == Of the four Utaki on Kamiji Island, Higashi Utaki is the oldest and is regarded as the second holiest site after {{ill|Bi Utaki|ja|美御嶽|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bi Utaki|qq=}} (Naha-ugan). It is located east (Āru) of the only settlement on Kamiji Island and enshrines the deity of [[:en:Ryūgū-jō|Ryūgū]]<ref name="hesiki_1975" /><ref name="okinawastory" />. The site is situated on a rocky plateau, and behind the worship area is the remains of an enclosure surrounded by stone walls<ref name="shojiten" />. Fragments of {{ill|Panari pottery|ja|パナリ焼き|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Panari pottery|qq=}} have been found around the site<ref name="shojiten" />.
Access to the Utaki is restricted, with a "No Entry" sign posted on the path leading to it. Only designated shrine caretakers are permitted to enter beyond the torii gate<ref name="okinawatimes_1999-12-18">{{cite news|ja | title = Island Life: Seafarers' Living (8) Aragusuku Island (Taketomi Town) | url = http://www.okinawatimes.co.jp/spe/yuku991218.html | newspaper = Okinawa Times | date = 1999-12-18 | archiveurl = https://web.archive.org/web/20000901000807/http://www.okinawatimes.co.jp/spe/yuku991218.html | archivedate = 2000-09-01}}</ref>.
== History == Higashi Utaki is also referred to as the "Original Utaki" (Mutuuugan), as it is believed to have been established when people first settled on Kamiji Island<ref name="hesiki_1975" />. Though the history of the island remains unclear, records from [[1477]] indicate that three shipwrecked Koreans from [[Jeju Island]] were rescued and repatriated through Aragusuku, confirming that the island had a settled population at that time. During the era when the [[:en:Ryukyu Kingdom|Ryukyu Kingdom]] imposed the [[:en:Poll tax|Head Tax]], Aragusuku Island, which had limited fresh water and farmland, was required to provide dried [[:en:Dugong|Dugong]] meat as tribute. The skulls of captured dugongs were offered at Higashi Utaki as part of prayers for abundant marine harvests. At one time, a large number of dugong skulls were piled at the site, but theft and other factors have significantly reduced their number. The name "Zannuon" means "Dugong Utaki," and "Iso" in "Iso Utaki" can also mean "sea".
== Rituals == Higashi Utaki is a focal point for prayers during the three major festivals of the island: the Harvest Festival (Buru), the Seasonal Festival (Siichi), and the Kichugan Festival (Kichugan). The {{ill|Kaminchu|ja|神司|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaminchu|qq=}} (priestess) responsible for the shrine traditionally comes from the Asato family (Āru Asadei), while the {{ill|Kamanga|ja|カマンガー|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamanga|qq=}} (assistant male priest) comes from the Shin family (Āraei). As of 2013, the shrine has no serving priestess. == References ==
== Bibliography ==
* {{cite book|ja|title=Taketomi Town History Volume 5: Aragusuku Island|editor=Taketomi Town History Compilation Committee|date=2013-11-30|ref={{SfnRef|町史|2013}}}}

During the era when the [[:en:Ryukyu Kingdom|Ryukyu Kingdom]] imposed the [[:en:Poll tax|Head Tax]], Aragusuku Island, which had limited fresh water and farmland, was required to provide dried [[:en:Dugong|Dugong]] meat as tribute. The skulls of captured dugongs were offered at Higashi Utaki as part of prayers for abundant marine harvests. At one time, a large number of dugong skulls were piled at the site, but theft and other factors have significantly reduced their number. The name "Zannuon" means "Dugong Utaki," and "Iso" in "Iso Utaki" can also mean "sea".
== Rituals == Higashi Utaki is a focal point for prayers during the three major festivals of the island: the Harvest Festival (Buru), the Seasonal Festival (Siichi), and the Kichugan Festival (Kichugan). The {{ill|Kaminchu|ja|神司|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaminchu|qq=}} (priestess) responsible for the shrine traditionally comes from the Asato family (Āru Asadei), while the {{ill|Kamanga|ja|カマンガー|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamanga|qq=}} (assistant male priest) comes from the Shin family (Āraei). As of 2013, the shrine has no serving priestess. == References ==

{{infobox religious building |name=Ārīugan }} '''Higashi Utaki''' (東御嶽, Āruugan, Ārīugan<ref name="okinawastory">{{Cite web|ja | title = Aragusuku Island | url = https://www.okinawastory.jp/spot/1304 | work = Okinawa Story | publisher = Okinawa Convention & Visitors Bureau | accessdate = 2023-05-14 | archiveurl = https://web.archive.org/web/20221128013234/https://www.okinawastory.jp/spot/1304 | archivedate = 2022-11-28}}</ref>) is an [[:en:Ontake|Utaki]] located on [[:en:Aragusuku Islands|Aragusuku Island]] (Kamiji Island), in [[:en:Taketomi, Okinawa|Taketomi, Okinawa]], [[:en:Okinawa Prefecture|Okinawa Prefecture]]. It is also known as '''Iso Utaki''' (磯御嶽, Ishāugan, Ishōugan), '''Gusuku Utaki''' (グスクウガン, Kūsukuugan<ref name="hesiki_1975">https://hdl.handle.net/2308/1372 </ref>), and '''Zannuon'''<ref name="shojiten">{{cite book|ja | title = Yaeyama Small Encyclopedia | page = 91 | author = Tsuneshin Sakihara | publisher = Border Ink | date = 1999-08-01}}</ref>. It is sometimes colloquially referred to as the "Mermaid Shrine" (人魚神社, Ningyo Jinja), though it is not a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]]<ref>{{cite encyclopedia|ja | title = Aragusuku Island | encyclopedia= Kadokawa Japan Place Name Encyclopedia 47 Okinawa Prefecture | author = Kadokawa Japan Place Name Compilation Committee | date= 1986-07-08 | publisher= Kadokawa Shoten | page = 130}}</ref>.
== Overview == Of the four Utaki on Kamiji Island, Higashi Utaki is the oldest and is regarded as the second holiest site after {{ill|Bi Utaki|ja|美御嶽|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bi Utaki|qq=}} (Naha-ugan). It is located east (Āru) of the only settlement on Kamiji Island and enshrines the deity of [[:en:Ryūgū-jō|Ryūgū]]<ref name="hesiki_1975" /><ref name="okinawastory" />. The site is situated on a rocky plateau, and behind the worship area is the remains of an enclosure surrounded by stone walls<ref name="shojiten" />. Fragments of {{ill|Panari pottery|ja|パナリ焼き|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Panari pottery|qq=}} have been found around the site<ref name="shojiten" />.
Access to the Utaki is restricted, with a "No Entry" sign posted on the path leading to it. Only designated shrine caretakers are permitted to enter beyond the torii gate<ref name="okinawatimes_1999-12-18">{{cite news|ja | title = Island Life: Seafarers' Living (8) Aragusuku Island (Taketomi Town) | url = http://www.okinawatimes.co.jp/spe/yuku991218.html | newspaper = Okinawa Times | date = 1999-12-18 | archiveurl = https://web.archive.org/web/20000901000807/http://www.okinawatimes.co.jp/spe/yuku991218.html | archivedate = 2000-09-01}}</ref>.
== History == Higashi Utaki is also referred to as the "Original Utaki" (Mutuuugan), as it is believed to have been established when people first settled on Kamiji Island<ref name="hesiki_1975" />. Though the history of the island remains unclear, records from [[:en:1477|1477]] indicate that three shipwrecked Koreans from [[Jeju Island]] were rescued and repatriated through Aragusuku, confirming that the island had a settled population at that time. During the era when the [[:en:Ryukyu Kingdom|Ryukyu Kingdom]] imposed the [[:en:Poll tax|Head Tax]], Aragusuku Island, which had limited fresh water and farmland, was required to provide dried [[:en:Dugong|Dugong]] meat as tribute. The skulls of captured dugongs were offered at Higashi Utaki as part of prayers for abundant marine harvests. At one time, a large number of dugong skulls were piled at the site, but theft and other factors have significantly reduced their number. The name "Zannuon" means "Dugong Utaki," and "Iso" in "Iso Utaki" can also mean "sea".
== Rituals == Higashi Utaki is a focal point for prayers during the three major festivals of the island: the Harvest Festival (Buru), the Seasonal Festival (Siichi), and the Kichugan Festival (Kichugan). The {{ill|Kaminchu|ja|神司|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaminchu|qq=}} (priestess) responsible for the shrine traditionally comes from the Asato family (Āru Asadei), while the {{ill|Kamanga|ja|カマンガー|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamanga|qq=}} (assistant male priest) comes from the Shin family (Āraei). As of 2013, the shrine has no serving priestess. == References ==
== Bibliography ==

revision.text:
{{infobox religious building |name=Ārīugan }} '''Higashi Utaki''' (東御嶽, Āruugan, Ārīugan<ref name="okinawastory">{{Cite web|ja | title = Aragusuku Island | url = https://www.okinawastory.jp/spot/1304 | work = Okinawa Story | publisher = Okinawa Convention & Visitors Bureau | accessdate = 2023-05-14 | archiveurl = https://web.archive.org/web/20221128013234/https://www.okinawastory.jp/spot/1304 | archivedate = 2022-11-28}}</ref>) is an [[:en:Ontake|Utaki]] located on [[:en:Aragusuku Islands|Aragusuku Island]] (Kamiji Island), in [[:en:Taketomi, Okinawa|Taketomi, Okinawa]], [[:en:Okinawa Prefecture|Okinawa Prefecture]]. It is also known as '''Iso Utaki''' (磯御嶽, Ishāugan, Ishōugan), '''Gusuku Utaki''' (グスクウガン, Kūsukuugan<ref name="hesiki_1975">https://hdl.handle.net/2308/1372 </ref>), and '''Zannuon'''<ref name="shojiten">{{cite book|ja | title = Yaeyama Small Encyclopedia | page = 91 | author = Tsuneshin Sakihara | publisher = Border Ink | date = 1999-08-01}}</ref>. It is sometimes colloquially referred to as the "Mermaid Shrine" (人魚神社, Ningyo Jinja), though it is not a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]]<ref>{{cite encyclopedia|ja | title = Aragusuku Island | encyclopedia= Kadokawa Japan Place Name Encyclopedia 47 Okinawa Prefecture | author = Kadokawa Japan Place Name Compilation Committee | date= 1986-07-08 | publisher= Kadokawa Shoten | page = 130}}</ref>.
== Overview == Of the four Utaki on Kamiji Island, Higashi Utaki is the oldest and is regarded as the second holiest site after {{ill|Bi Utaki|ja|美御嶽|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bi Utaki|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Naha-ugan). It is located east (Āru) of the only settlement on Kamiji Island and enshrines the deity of [[:en:Ryūgū-jō|Ryūgū]]<ref name="hesiki_1975" /><ref name="okinawastory" />. The site is situated on a rocky plateau, and behind the worship area is the remains of an enclosure surrounded by stone walls<ref name="shojiten" />. Fragments of {{ill|Panari pottery|ja|パナリ焼き|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Panari pottery|qq=}} have been found around the site<ref name="shojiten" />.
Access to the Utaki is restricted, with a "No Entry" sign posted on the path leading to it. Only designated shrine caretakers are permitted to enter beyond the torii gate<ref name="okinawatimes_1999-12-18">{{cite news|ja | title = Island Life: Seafarers' Living (8) Aragusuku Island (Taketomi Town) | url = http://www.okinawatimes.co.jp/spe/yuku991218.html | newspaper = Okinawa Times | date = 1999-12-18 | archiveurl = https://web.archive.org/web/20000901000807/http://www.okinawatimes.co.jp/spe/yuku991218.html | archivedate = 2000-09-01}}</ref>.
== History == Higashi Utaki is also referred to as the "Original Utaki" (Mutuuugan), as it is believed to have been established when people first settled on Kamiji Island<ref name="hesiki_1975" />. Though the history of the island remains unclear, records from [[:en:1477|1477]] indicate that three shipwrecked Koreans from [[Jeju Island]] were rescued and repatriated through Aragusuku, confirming that the island had a settled population at that time. During the era when the [[:en:Ryukyu Kingdom|Ryukyu Kingdom]] imposed the [[:en:Poll tax|Head Tax]], Aragusuku Island, which had limited fresh water and farmland, was required to provide dried [[:en:Dugong|Dugong]] meat as tribute. The skulls of captured dugongs were offered at Higashi Utaki as part of prayers for abundant marine harvests. At one time, a large number of dugong skulls were piled at the site, but theft and other factors have significantly reduced their number. The name "Zannuon" means "Dugong Utaki," and "Iso" in "Iso Utaki" can also mean "sea".
== Rituals == Higashi Utaki is a focal point for prayers during the three major festivals of the island: the Harvest Festival (Buru), the Seasonal Festival (Siichi), and the Kichugan Festival (Kichugan). The {{ill|Kaminchu|ja|神司|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaminchu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (priestess) responsible for the shrine traditionally comes from the Asato family (Āru Asadei), while the {{ill|Kamanga|ja|カマンガー|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamanga|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (assistant male priest) comes from the Shin family (Āraei). As of 2013, the shrine has no serving priestess. == References ==
== Bibliography ==
* {{cite book|ja|title=Taketomi Town History Volume 5: Aragusuku Island|editor=Taketomi Town History Compilation Committee|date=2013-11-30|ref={{SfnRef|町史|2013}}}}
== See also ==
* {{ill|Akamata and Kuromata|ja|アカマタ・クロマタ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akamata and Kuromata|qq=}} {{coord|24|18|15.07|N|123|53|49.68|E|type:landmark_region:JP-47|display=title}} {{Reli-stub}} {{Pref-stub|pref=沖縄県}} {{Okinawan Shrines}}
{{translated page|ja|東御嶽 (竹富町新城上地)|version=98122603|comment=Imported full ja history}}
[[Category:Utaki (Okinawa)]] [[Category:Buildings in Taketomi Town]] [[Category:History of Taketomi Town]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[ja:東御嶽 (竹富町新城上地)]] [[sn:Ārīugan]] [[ja:Ārīugan]] [[Category:Utaki (Okinawa)]]

Only designated shrine caretakers are permitted to enter beyond the torii gate<ref name="okinawatimes_1999-12-18">{{cite news|ja | title = Island Life: Seafarers' Living (8) Aragusuku Island (Taketomi Town) | url = http://www.okinawatimes.co.jp/spe/yuku991218.html | newspaper = Okinawa Times | date = 1999-12-18 | archiveurl = https://web.archive.org/web/20000901000807/http://www.okinawatimes.co.jp/spe/yuku991218.html | archivedate = 2000-09-01}}</ref>.
== History == Higashi Utaki is also referred to as the "Original Utaki" (Mutuuugan), as it is believed to have been established when people first settled on Kamiji Island<ref name="hesiki_1975" />. Though the history of the island remains unclear, records from [[:en:1477|1477]] indicate that three shipwrecked Koreans from [[:en:Jeju Island|Jeju Island]] were rescued and repatriated through Aragusuku, confirming that the island had a settled population at that time. During the era when the [[:en:Ryukyu Kingdom|Ryukyu Kingdom]] imposed the [[:en:Poll tax|Head Tax]], Aragusuku Island, which had limited fresh water and farmland, was required to provide dried [[:en:Dugong|Dugong]] meat as tribute. The skulls of captured dugongs were offered at Higashi Utaki as part of prayers for abundant marine harvests. At one time, a large number of dugong skulls were piled at the site, but theft and other factors have significantly reduced their number. The name "Zannuon" means "Dugong Utaki," and "Iso" in "Iso Utaki" can also mean "sea".
== Rituals == Higashi Utaki is a focal point for prayers during the three major festivals of the island: the Harvest Festival (Buru), the Seasonal Festival (Siichi), and the Kichugan Festival (Kichugan). The {{ill|Kaminchu|ja|神司|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaminchu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (priestess) responsible for the shrine traditionally comes from the Asato family (Āru Asadei), while the {{ill|Kamanga|ja|カマンガー|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamanga|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (assistant male priest) comes from the Shin family (Āraei). As of 2013, the shrine has no serving priestess. == References ==

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:33, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:36, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{infobox religious building |name=Ārīugan }} '''Higashi Utaki''' (東御嶽, Āruugan, Ārīugan<ref name="okinawastory">{{Cite web|ja | title = Aragusuku Island | url = https://www.okinawastory.jp/spot/1304 | work = Okinawa Story | publisher = Okinawa Convention & Visitors Bureau | accessdate = 2023-05-14 | archiveurl = https://web.archive.org/web/20221128013234/https://www.okinawastory.jp/spot/1304 | archivedate = 2022-11-28}}</ref>) is an [[:en:Ontake|Utaki]] located on [[:en:Aragusuku Islands|Aragusuku Island]] (Kamiji Island), in [[:en:Taketomi, Okinawa|Taketomi, Okinawa]], [[:en:Okinawa Prefecture|Okinawa Prefecture]]. It is also known as '''Iso Utaki''' (磯御嶽, Ishāugan, Ishōugan), '''Gusuku Utaki''' (グスクウガン, Kūsukuugan<ref name="hesiki_1975">https://hdl.handle.net/2308/1372 </ref>), and '''Zannuon'''<ref name="shojiten">{{cite book|ja | title = Yaeyama Small Encyclopedia | page = 91 | author = Tsuneshin Sakihara | publisher = Border Ink | date = 1999-08-01}}</ref>. It is sometimes colloquially referred to as the "Mermaid Shrine" (人魚神社, Ningyo Jinja), though it is not a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]]<ref>{{cite encyclopedia|ja | title = Aragusuku Island | encyclopedia= Kadokawa Japan Place Name Encyclopedia 47 Okinawa Prefecture | author = Kadokawa Japan Place Name Compilation Committee | date= 1986-07-08 | publisher= Kadokawa Shoten | page = 130}}</ref>.
== Overview == Of the four Utaki on Kamiji Island, Higashi Utaki is the oldest and is regarded as the second holiest site after {{ill|Bi Utaki|ja|美御嶽|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bi Utaki|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Naha-ugan). It is located east (Āru) of the only settlement on Kamiji Island and enshrines the deity of [[:en:Ryūgū-jō|Ryūgū]]<ref name="hesiki_1975" /><ref name="okinawastory" />. The site is situated on a rocky plateau, and behind the worship area is the remains of an enclosure surrounded by stone walls<ref name="shojiten" />. Fragments of {{ill|Panari pottery|ja|パナリ焼き|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Panari pottery|qq=}} have been found around the site<ref name="shojiten" />.
Access to the Utaki is restricted, with a "No Entry" sign posted on the path leading to it. During the era when the [[:en:Ryukyu Kingdom|Ryukyu Kingdom]] imposed the [[:en:Poll tax|Head Tax]], Aragusuku Island, which had limited fresh water and farmland, was required to provide dried [[:en:Dugong|Dugong]] meat as tribute. The skulls of captured dugongs were offered at Higashi Utaki as part of prayers for abundant marine harvests. At one time, a large number of dugong skulls were piled at the site, but theft and other factors have significantly reduced their number. The name "Zannuon" means "Dugong Utaki," and "Iso" in "Iso Utaki" can also mean "sea".
== Rituals == Higashi Utaki is a focal point for prayers during the three major festivals of the island: the Harvest Festival (Buru), the Seasonal Festival (Siichi), and the Kichugan Festival (Kichugan). The {{ill|Kaminchu|ja|神司|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaminchu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (priestess) responsible for the shrine traditionally comes from the Asato family (Āru Asadei), while the {{ill|Kamanga|ja|カマンガー|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamanga|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (assistant male priest) comes from the Shin family (Āraei). As of 2013, the shrine has no serving priestess. == References ==

The {{ill|Kaminchu|ja|神司|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaminchu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (priestess) responsible for the shrine traditionally comes from the Asato family (Āru Asadei), while the {{ill|Kamanga|ja|カマンガー|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamanga|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (assistant male priest) comes from the Shin family (Āraei). It is also known as '''Iso Utaki''' (磯御嶽, Ishāugan, Ishōugan), '''Gusuku Utaki''' (グスクウガン, Kūsukuugan<ref name="hesiki_1975">https://hdl.handle.net/2308/1372 </ref>), and '''Zannuon'''<ref name="shojiten">{{cite book|ja | title = Yaeyama Small Encyclopedia | page = 91 | author = Tsuneshin Sakihara | publisher = Border Ink | date = 1999-08-01}}</ref>. It is sometimes colloquially referred to as the "Mermaid Shrine" (人魚神社, Ningyo Jinja), though it is not a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]]<ref>{{cite encyclopedia|ja | title = Aragusuku Island | encyclopedia= Kadokawa Japan Place Name Encyclopedia 47 Okinawa Prefecture | author = Kadokawa Japan Place Name Compilation Committee | date= 1986-07-08 | publisher= Kadokawa Shoten | page = 130}}</ref>.
== Overview == Of the four Utaki on Kamiji Island, Higashi Utaki is the oldest and is regarded as the second holiest site after {{ill|Bi Utaki|ja|美御嶽|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bi Utaki|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Naha-ugan). It is located east (Āru) of the only settlement on Kamiji Island and enshrines the deity of [[:en:Ryūgū-jō|Ryūgū]]<ref name="hesiki_1975" /><ref name="okinawastory" />. The site is situated on a rocky plateau, and behind the worship area is the remains of an enclosure surrounded by stone walls<ref name="shojiten" />. Fragments of {{ill|Panari pottery|ja|パナリ焼き|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Panari pottery|qq=}} have been found around the site<ref name="shojiten" />.
Access to the Utaki is restricted, with a "No Entry" sign posted on the path leading to it. Only designated shrine caretakers are permitted to enter beyond the torii gate<ref name="okinawatimes_1999-12-18">{{cite news|ja | title = Island Life: Seafarers' Living (8) Aragusuku Island (Taketomi Town) | url = http://www.okinawatimes.co.jp/spe/yuku991218.html | newspaper = Okinawa Times | date = 1999-12-18 | archiveurl = https://web.archive.org/web/20000901000807/http://www.okinawatimes.co.jp/spe/yuku991218.html | archivedate = 2000-09-01}}</ref>.
== History == Higashi Utaki is also referred to as the "Original Utaki" (Mutuuugan), as it is believed to have been established when people first settled on Kamiji Island<ref name="hesiki_1975" />. Though the history of the island remains unclear, records from [[:en:1477|1477]] indicate that three shipwrecked Koreans from [[:en:Jeju Island|Jeju Island]] were rescued and repatriated through Aragusuku, confirming that the island had a settled population at that time. During the era when the [[:en:Ryukyu Kingdom|Ryukyu Kingdom]] imposed the [[:en:Poll tax|Head Tax]], Aragusuku Island, which had limited fresh water and farmland, was required to provide dried [[:en:Dugong|Dugong]] meat as tribute. The skulls of captured dugongs were offered at Higashi Utaki as part of prayers for abundant marine harvests. At one time, a large number of dugong skulls were piled at the site, but theft and other factors have significantly reduced their number. The name "Zannuon" means "Dugong Utaki," and "Iso" in "Iso Utaki" can also mean "sea".
== Rituals == Higashi Utaki is a focal point for prayers during the three major festivals of the island: the Harvest Festival (Buru), the Seasonal Festival (Siichi), and the Kichugan Festival (Kichugan). The {{ill|Kaminchu|ja|神司|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaminchu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (priestess) responsible for the shrine traditionally comes from the Asato family (Āru Asadei), while the {{ill|Kamanga|ja|カマンガー|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamanga|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (assistant male priest) comes from the Shin family (Āraei). As of 2013, the shrine has no serving priestess.

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q134963148]]Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:33, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:36, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{infobox religious building |name=Ārīugan }} {{Nihongo|'''Ārīugan'''|東御嶽}}<ref name="okinawastory">{{Cite web|ja | title = Aragusuku Island | url = https://www.okinawastory.jp/spot/1304 | work = Okinawa Story | publisher = Okinawa Convention & Visitors Bureau | accessdate = 2023-05-14 | archiveurl = https://web.archive.org/web/20221128013234/https://www.okinawastory.jp/spot/1304 | archivedate = 2022-11-28}}</ref> is an [[:en:Ontake|Utaki]] located on [[:en:Aragusuku Islands|Aragusuku Island]] (Kamiji Island), in [[:en:Taketomi, Okinawa|Taketomi, Okinawa]], [[:en:Okinawa Prefecture|Okinawa Prefecture]]. It is also known as '''Iso Utaki''' (磯御嶽, Ishāugan, Ishōugan), '''Gusuku Utaki''' (グスクウガン, Kūsukuugan<ref name="hesiki_1975">https://hdl.handle.net/2308/1372 </ref>), and '''Zannuon'''<ref name="shojiten">{{cite book|ja | title = Yaeyama Small Encyclopedia | page = 91 | author = Tsuneshin Sakihara | publisher = Border Ink | date = 1999-08-01}}</ref>. It is sometimes colloquially referred to as the "Mermaid Shrine" (人魚神社, Ningyo Jinja), though it is not a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]]<ref>{{cite encyclopedia|ja | title = Aragusuku Island | encyclopedia= Kadokawa Japan Place Name Encyclopedia 47 Okinawa Prefecture | author = Kadokawa Japan Place Name Compilation Committee | date= 1986-07-08 | publisher= Kadokawa Shoten | page = 130}}</ref>.
== Overview == Of the four Utaki on Kamiji Island, Higashi Utaki is the oldest and is regarded as the second holiest site after {{ill|Bi Utaki|ja|美御嶽|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bi Utaki|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Naha-ugan). It is located east (Āru) of the only settlement on Kamiji Island and enshrines the deity of [[:en:Ryūgū-jō|Ryūgū]]<ref name="hesiki_1975" /><ref name="okinawastory" />. The site is situated on a rocky plateau, and behind the worship area is the remains of an enclosure surrounded by stone walls<ref name="shojiten" />. Fragments of {{ill|Panari pottery|ja|パナリ焼き|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Panari pottery|qq=|1=Panari ware|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11329177}} have been found around the site<ref name="shojiten" />.
Access to the Utaki is restricted, with a "No Entry" sign posted on the path leading to it. Only designated shrine caretakers are permitted to enter beyond the torii gate<ref name="okinawatimes_1999-12-18">{{cite news|ja | title = Island Life: Seafarers' Living (8) Aragusuku Island (Taketomi Town) | url = http://www.okinawatimes.co.jp/spe/yuku991218.html | newspaper = Okinawa Times | date = 1999-12-18 | archiveurl = https://web.archive.org/web/20000901000807/http://www.okinawatimes.co.jp/spe/yuku991218.html | archivedate = 2000-09-01}}</ref>.
== History == Higashi Utaki is also referred to as the "Original Utaki" (Mutuuugan), as it is believed to have been established when people first settled on Kamiji Island<ref name="hesiki_1975" />. Though the history of the island remains unclear, records from [[:en:1477|1477]] indicate that three shipwrecked Koreans from [[:en:Jeju Island|Jeju Island]] were rescued and repatriated through Aragusuku, confirming that the island had a settled population at that time. During the era when the [[:en:Ryukyu Kingdom|Ryukyu Kingdom]] imposed the [[:en:Poll tax|Head Tax]], Aragusuku Island, which had limited fresh water and farmland, was required to provide dried [[:en:Dugong|Dugong]] meat as tribute. The skulls of captured dugongs were offered at Higashi Utaki as part of prayers for abundant marine harvests. At one time, a large number of dugong skulls were piled at the site, but theft and other factors have significantly reduced their number. The name "Zannuon" means "Dugong Utaki," and "Iso" in "Iso Utaki" can also mean "sea".
== Rituals == Higashi Utaki is a focal point for prayers during the three major festivals of the island: the Harvest Festival (Buru), the Seasonal Festival (Siichi), and the Kichugan Festival (Kichugan). The {{ill|Kaminchu|ja|神司|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaminchu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (priestess) responsible for the shrine traditionally comes from the Asato family (Āru Asadei), while the {{ill|Kamanga|ja|カマンガー|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamanga|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (assistant male priest) comes from the Shin family (Āraei). As of 2013, the shrine has no serving priestess.
== See also ==
* {{ill|Akamata and Kuromata|ja|アカマタ・クロマタ|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akamata and Kuromata|1=Akamata and Kuromata|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11282723}} {{coord|24|18|15.07|N|123|53|49.68|E|type:landmark_region:JP-47|display=title}} == References == <references />
== Bibliography ==

[[Category:Utaki (Okinawa)]] [[Category:Buildings in Taketomi Town]] [[Category:History of Taketomi Town]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[ja:東御嶽 (竹富町新城上地)]] [[sn:Ārīugan]] [[ja:Ārīugan]] [[Category:Utaki (Okinawa)]] {{wikidata link|Q134963148}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:東御嶽 (竹富町新城上地)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q134963148]]Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:33, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:36, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{infobox religious building |name=Ārīugan }} {{Nihongo|'''Ārīugan'''|東御嶽}}<ref name="okinawastory">{{Cite web|ja | title = Aragusuku Island | url = https://www.okinawastory.jp/spot/1304 | work = Okinawa Story | publisher = Okinawa Convention & Visitors Bureau | accessdate = 2023-05-14 | archiveurl = https://web.archive.org/web/20221128013234/https://www.okinawastory.jp/spot/1304 | archivedate = 2022-11-28}}</ref> is an [[:en:Ontake|Utaki]] located on [[:en:Aragusuku Islands|Aragusuku Island]] (Kamiji Island), in [[:en:Taketomi, Okinawa|Taketomi, Okinawa]], [[:en:Okinawa Prefecture|Okinawa Prefecture]]. It is also known as '''Iso Utaki''' (磯御嶽, Ishāugan, Ishōugan), '''Gusuku Utaki''' (グスクウガン, Kūsukuugan<ref name="hesiki_1975">https://hdl.handle.net/2308/1372 </ref>), and '''Zannuon'''<ref name="shojiten">{{cite book|ja | title = Yaeyama Small Encyclopedia | page = 91 | author = Tsuneshin Sakihara | publisher = Border Ink | date = 1999-08-01}}</ref>. It is sometimes colloquially referred to as the "Mermaid Shrine" (人魚神社, Ningyo Jinja), though it is not a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]]<ref>{{cite encyclopedia|ja | title = Aragusuku Island | encyclopedia= Kadokawa Japan Place Name Encyclopedia 47 Okinawa Prefecture | author = Kadokawa Japan Place Name Compilation Committee | date= 1986-07-08 | publisher= Kadokawa Shoten | page = 130}}</ref>.
== Overview == Of the four Utaki on Kamiji Island, Higashi Utaki is the oldest and is regarded as the second holiest site after {{ill|Bi Utaki|ja|美御嶽|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bi Utaki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Naha-ugan). It is located east (Āru) of the only settlement on Kamiji Island and enshrines the deity of [[:en:Ryūgū-jō|Ryūgū]]<ref name="hesiki_1975" /><ref name="okinawastory" />. The site is situated on a rocky plateau, and behind the worship area is the remains of an enclosure surrounded by stone walls<ref name="shojiten" />. Fragments of {{ill|Panari pottery|ja|パナリ焼き|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Panari pottery|1=Panari ware|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11329177}} have been found around the site<ref name="shojiten" />.
Access to the Utaki is restricted, with a "No Entry" sign posted on the path leading to it. Only designated shrine caretakers are permitted to enter beyond the torii gate<ref name="okinawatimes_1999-12-18">{{cite news|ja | title = Island Life: Seafarers' Living (8) Aragusuku Island (Taketomi Town) | url = http://www.okinawatimes.co.jp/spe/yuku991218.html | newspaper = Okinawa Times | date = 1999-12-18 | archiveurl = https://web.archive.org/web/20000901000807/http://www.okinawatimes.co.jp/spe/yuku991218.html | archivedate = 2000-09-01}}</ref>.
== History == Higashi Utaki is also referred to as the "Original Utaki" (Mutuuugan), as it is believed to have been established when people first settled on Kamiji Island<ref name="hesiki_1975" />. Though the history of the island remains unclear, records from [[:en:1477|1477]] indicate that three shipwrecked Koreans from [[:en:Jeju Island|Jeju Island]] were rescued and repatriated through Aragusuku, confirming that the island had a settled population at that time. During the era when the [[:en:Ryukyu Kingdom|Ryukyu Kingdom]] imposed the [[:en:Poll tax|Head Tax]], Aragusuku Island, which had limited fresh water and farmland, was required to provide dried [[:en:Dugong|Dugong]] meat as tribute. The skulls of captured dugongs were offered at Higashi Utaki as part of prayers for abundant marine harvests. At one time, a large number of dugong skulls were piled at the site, but theft and other factors have significantly reduced their number. The name "Zannuon" means "Dugong Utaki," and "Iso" in "Iso Utaki" can also mean "sea".
== Rituals == Higashi Utaki is a focal point for prayers during the three major festivals of the island: the Harvest Festival (Buru), the Seasonal Festival (Siichi), and the Kichugan Festival (Kichugan). The {{ill|Kaminchu|ja|神司|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaminchu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (priestess) responsible for the shrine traditionally comes from the Asato family (Āru Asadei), while the {{ill|Kamanga|ja|カマンガー|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamanga|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (assistant male priest) comes from the Shin family (Āraei). As of 2013, the shrine has no serving priestess.
== See also ==

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:東御嶽 (竹富町新城上地)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q134963148]]Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:33, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:36, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{infobox religious building |name=Ārīugan }} {{Nihongo|'''Ārīugan'''|東御嶽}}<ref name="okinawastory">{{Cite web|ja | title = Aragusuku Island | url = https://www.okinawastory.jp/spot/1304 | work = Okinawa Story | publisher = Okinawa Convention & Visitors Bureau | accessdate = 2023-05-14 | archiveurl = https://web.archive.org/web/20221128013234/https://www.okinawastory.jp/spot/1304 | archivedate = 2022-11-28}}</ref> is an [[:en:Ontake|Utaki]] located on [[:en:Aragusuku Islands|Aragusuku Island]] (Kamiji Island), in [[:en:Taketomi, Okinawa|Taketomi, Okinawa]], [[:en:Okinawa Prefecture|Okinawa Prefecture]]. It is also known as '''Iso Utaki''' (磯御嶽, Ishāugan, Ishōugan), '''Gusuku Utaki''' (グスクウガン, Kūsukuugan<ref name="hesiki_1975">https://hdl.handle.net/2308/1372 </ref>), and '''Zannuon'''<ref name="shojiten">{{cite book|ja | title = Yaeyama Small Encyclopedia | page = 91 | author = Tsuneshin Sakihara | publisher = Border Ink | date = 1999-08-01}}</ref>. It is sometimes colloquially referred to as the "Mermaid Shrine" (人魚神社, Ningyo Jinja), though it is not a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]]<ref>{{cite encyclopedia|ja | title = Aragusuku Island | encyclopedia= Kadokawa Japan Place Name Encyclopedia 47 Okinawa Prefecture | author = Kadokawa Japan Place Name Compilation Committee | date= 1986-07-08 | publisher= Kadokawa Shoten | page = 130}}</ref>.
== Overview == Of the four Utaki on Kamiji Island, Higashi Utaki is the oldest and is regarded as the second holiest site after {{ill|Bi Utaki|ja|美御嶽|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bi Utaki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Naha-ugan). It is located east (Āru) of the only settlement on Kamiji Island and enshrines the deity of [[:en:Ryūgū-jō|Ryūgū]]<ref name="hesiki_1975" /><ref name="okinawastory" />. The site is situated on a rocky plateau, and behind the worship area is the remains of an enclosure surrounded by stone walls<ref name="shojiten" />. Fragments of {{ill|Panari pottery|ja|パナリ焼き|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Panari pottery|1=Panari ware|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11329177}} have been found around the site<ref name="shojiten" />.
Access to the Utaki is restricted, with a "No Entry" sign posted on the path leading to it. Only designated shrine caretakers are permitted to enter beyond the torii gate<ref name="okinawatimes_1999-12-18">{{cite news|ja | title = Island Life: Seafarers' Living (8) Aragusuku Island (Taketomi Town) | url = http://www.okinawatimes.co.jp/spe/yuku991218.html | newspaper = Okinawa Times | date = 1999-12-18 | archiveurl = https://web.archive.org/web/20000901000807/http://www.okinawatimes.co.jp/spe/yuku991218.html | archivedate = 2000-09-01}}</ref>.
== History == Higashi Utaki is also referred to as the "Original Utaki" (Mutuuugan), as it is believed to have been established when people first settled on Kamiji Island<ref name="hesiki_1975" />. Though the history of the island remains unclear, records from [[:en:1477|1477]] indicate that three shipwrecked Koreans from [[:en:Jeju Island|Jeju Island]] were rescued and repatriated through Aragusuku, confirming that the island had a settled population at that time. During the era when the [[:en:Ryukyu Kingdom|Ryukyu Kingdom]] imposed the [[:en:Poll tax|Head Tax]], Aragusuku Island, which had limited fresh water and farmland, was required to provide dried [[:en:Dugong|Dugong]] meat as tribute. The skulls of captured dugongs were offered at Higashi Utaki as part of prayers for abundant marine harvests. At one time, a large number of dugong skulls were piled at the site, but theft and other factors have significantly reduced their number. The name "Zannuon" means "Dugong Utaki," and "Iso" in "Iso Utaki" can also mean "sea".
== Rituals == Higashi Utaki is a focal point for prayers during the three major festivals of the island: the Harvest Festival (Buru), the Seasonal Festival (Siichi), and the Kichugan Festival (Kichugan). The {{ill|Kaminchu|ja|神司|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaminchu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (priestess) responsible for the shrine traditionally comes from the Asato family (Āru Asadei), while the {{ill|Kamanga|ja|カマンガー|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamanga|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (assistant male priest) comes from the Shin family (Āraei). As of 2013, the shrine has no serving priestess.
