<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:東御嶽 (竹富町新城上地)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q134963148]]Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:33, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:36, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{infobox religious building |name=Ārīugan }} {{Nihongo|'''Ārīugan'''|東御嶽}}<ref name="okinawastory">{{Cite web|ja | title = Aragusuku Island | url = https://www.okinawastory.jp/spot/1304 | work = Okinawa Story | publisher = Okinawa Convention & Visitors Bureau | accessdate = 2023-05-14 | archiveurl = https://web.archive.org/web/20221128013234/https://www.okinawastory.jp/spot/1304 | archivedate = 2022-11-28}}</ref> is an [[:en:Ontake|Utaki]] located on [[:en:Aragusuku Islands|Aragusuku Island]] (Kamiji Island), in [[:en:Taketomi, Okinawa|Taketomi, Okinawa]], [[:en:Okinawa Prefecture|Okinawa Prefecture]]. It is also known as '''Iso Utaki''' (磯御嶽, Ishāugan, Ishōugan), '''Gusuku Utaki''' (グスクウガン, Kūsukuugan<ref name="hesiki_1975">https://hdl.handle.net/2308/1372 </ref>), and '''Zannuon'''<ref name="shojiten">{{cite book|ja | title = Yaeyama Small Encyclopedia | page = 91 | author = Tsuneshin Sakihara | publisher = Border Ink | date = 1999-08-01}}</ref>. It is sometimes colloquially referred to as the "Mermaid Shrine" (人魚神社, Ningyo Jinja), though it is not a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]]<ref>{{cite encyclopedia|ja | title = Aragusuku Island | encyclopedia= Kadokawa Japan Place Name Encyclopedia 47 Okinawa Prefecture | author = Kadokawa Japan Place Name Compilation Committee | date= 1986-07-08 | publisher= Kadokawa Shoten | page = 130}}</ref>.
== Overview == Of the four Utaki on Kamiji Island, Higashi Utaki is the oldest and is regarded as the second holiest site after {{ill|Bi Utaki|ja|美御嶽|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bi Utaki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Naha-ugan). It is located east (Āru) of the only settlement on Kamiji Island and enshrines the deity of [[:en:Ryūgū-jō|Ryūgū]]<ref name="hesiki_1975" /><ref name="okinawastory" />. The site is situated on a rocky plateau, and behind the worship area is the remains of an enclosure surrounded by stone walls<ref name="shojiten" />. Fragments of {{ill|Panari pottery|ja|パナリ焼き|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Panari pottery|1=Panari ware|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11329177}} have been found around the site<ref name="shojiten" />.
Access to the Utaki is restricted, with a "No Entry" sign posted on the path leading to it. Only designated shrine caretakers are permitted to enter beyond the torii gate<ref name="okinawatimes_1999-12-18">{{cite news|ja | title = Island Life: Seafarers' Living (8) Aragusuku Island (Taketomi Town) | url = http://www.okinawatimes.co.jp/spe/yuku991218.html | newspaper = Okinawa Times | date = 1999-12-18 | archiveurl = https://web.archive.org/web/20000901000807/http://www.okinawatimes.co.jp/spe/yuku991218.html | archivedate = 2000-09-01}}</ref>.
== History == Higashi Utaki is also referred to as the "Original Utaki" (Mutuuugan), as it is believed to have been established when people first settled on Kamiji Island<ref name="hesiki_1975" />. Though the history of the island remains unclear, records from [[:en:1477|1477]] indicate that three shipwrecked Koreans from [[:en:Jeju Island|Jeju Island]] were rescued and repatriated through Aragusuku, confirming that the island had a settled population at that time. During the era when the [[:en:Ryukyu Kingdom|Ryukyu Kingdom]] imposed the [[:en:Poll tax|Head Tax]], Aragusuku Island, which had limited fresh water and farmland, was required to provide dried [[:en:Dugong|Dugong]] meat as tribute. The skulls of captured dugongs were offered at Higashi Utaki as part of prayers for abundant marine harvests. At one time, a large number of dugong skulls were piled at the site, but theft and other factors have significantly reduced their number. The name "Zannuon" means "Dugong Utaki," and "Iso" in "Iso Utaki" can also mean "sea".
== Rituals == Higashi Utaki is a focal point for prayers during the three major festivals of the island: the Harvest Festival (Buru), the Seasonal Festival (Siichi), and the Kichugan Festival (Kichugan). The {{ill|Kaminchu|ja|神司|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaminchu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (priestess) responsible for the shrine traditionally comes from the Asato family (Āru Asadei), while the {{ill|Kamanga|ja|カマンガー|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamanga|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (assistant male priest) comes from the Shin family (Āraei). As of 2013, the shrine has no serving priestess.
== See also ==
* {{ill|Akamata and Kuromata|ja|アカマタ・クロマタ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akamata and Kuromata|1=Akamata and Kuromata|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11282723}} {{coord|24|18|15.07|N|123|53|49.68|E|type:landmark_region:JP-47|display=title}} == References == <references />
== Bibliography ==
* {{cite book|ja|title=Taketomi Town History Volume 5: Aragusuku Island|editor=Taketomi Town History Compilation Committee|date=2013-11-30|ref={{SfnRef|町史|2013}}}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:東御嶽 (竹富町新城上地)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:東御嶽 (竹富町新城上地)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:東御嶽 (竹富町新城上地)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q134963148]]Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:33, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:36, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{infobox religious building |name=Ārīugan }} {{Nihongo|'''Ārīugan'''|東御嶽}}<ref name="okinawastory">{{Cite web|ja | title = Aragusuku Island | url = https://www.okinawastory.jp/spot/1304 | work = Okinawa Story | publisher = Okinawa Convention & Visitors Bureau | accessdate = 2023-05-14 | archiveurl = https://web.archive.org/web/20221128013234/https://www.okinawastory.jp/spot/1304 | archivedate = 2022-11-28}}</ref> is an [[:en:Ontake|Utaki]] located on [[:en:Aragusuku Islands|Aragusuku Island]] (Kamiji Island), in [[:en:Taketomi, Okinawa|Taketomi, Okinawa]], [[:en:Okinawa Prefecture|Okinawa Prefecture]]. It is also known as '''Iso Utaki''' (磯御嶽, Ishāugan, Ishōugan), '''Gusuku Utaki''' (グスクウガン, Kūsukuugan<ref name="hesiki_1975">https://hdl.handle.net/2308/1372 </ref>), and '''Zannuon'''<ref name="shojiten">{{cite book|ja | title = Yaeyama Small Encyclopedia | page = 91 | author = Tsuneshin Sakihara | publisher = Border Ink | date = 1999-08-01}}</ref>. It is sometimes colloquially referred to as the "Mermaid Shrine" (人魚神社, Ningyo Jinja), though it is not a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]]<ref>{{cite encyclopedia|ja | title = Aragusuku Island | encyclopedia= Kadokawa Japan Place Name Encyclopedia 47 Okinawa Prefecture | author = Kadokawa Japan Place Name Compilation Committee | date= 1986-07-08 | publisher= Kadokawa Shoten | page = 130}}</ref>.
== Overview == Of the four Utaki on Kamiji Island, Higashi Utaki is the oldest and is regarded as the second holiest site after {{ill|Bi Utaki|ja|美御嶽|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bi Utaki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Naha-ugan). It is located east (Āru) of the only settlement on Kamiji Island and enshrines the deity of [[:en:Ryūgū-jō|Ryūgū]]<ref name="hesiki_1975" /><ref name="okinawastory" />. The site is situated on a rocky plateau, and behind the worship area is the remains of an enclosure surrounded by stone walls<ref name="shojiten" />. Fragments of {{ill|Panari pottery|ja|パナリ焼き|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Panari pottery|1=Panari ware|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11329177}} have been found around the site<ref name="shojiten" />.
Access to the Utaki is restricted, with a "No Entry" sign posted on the path leading to it. Only designated shrine caretakers are permitted to enter beyond the torii gate<ref name="okinawatimes_1999-12-18">{{cite news|ja | title = Island Life: Seafarers' Living (8) Aragusuku Island (Taketomi Town) | url = http://www.okinawatimes.co.jp/spe/yuku991218.html | newspaper = Okinawa Times | date = 1999-12-18 | archiveurl = https://web.archive.org/web/20000901000807/http://www.okinawatimes.co.jp/spe/yuku991218.html | archivedate = 2000-09-01}}</ref>.
== History == Higashi Utaki is also referred to as the "Original Utaki" (Mutuuugan), as it is believed to have been established when people first settled on Kamiji Island<ref name="hesiki_1975" />. Though the history of the island remains unclear, records from [[:en:1477|1477]] indicate that three shipwrecked Koreans from [[:en:Jeju Island|Jeju Island]] were rescued and repatriated through Aragusuku, confirming that the island had a settled population at that time. During the era when the [[:en:Ryukyu Kingdom|Ryukyu Kingdom]] imposed the [[:en:Poll tax|Head Tax]], Aragusuku Island, which had limited fresh water and farmland, was required to provide dried [[:en:Dugong|Dugong]] meat as tribute. The skulls of captured dugongs were offered at Higashi Utaki as part of prayers for abundant marine harvests. At one time, a large number of dugong skulls were piled at the site, but theft and other factors have significantly reduced their number. The name "Zannuon" means "Dugong Utaki," and "Iso" in "Iso Utaki" can also mean "sea".
== Rituals == Higashi Utaki is a focal point for prayers during the three major festivals of the island: the Harvest Festival (Buru), the Seasonal Festival (Siichi), and the Kichugan Festival (Kichugan). The {{ill|Kaminchu|ja|神司|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaminchu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (priestess) responsible for the shrine traditionally comes from the Asato family (Āru Asadei), while the {{ill|Kamanga|ja|カマンガー|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamanga|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (assistant male priest) comes from the Shin family (Āraei). As of 2013, the shrine has no serving priestess.
== See also ==
* {{ill|Akamata and Kuromata|ja|アカマタ・クロマタ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akamata and Kuromata|1=Akamata and Kuromata|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11282723}} {{coord|24|18|15.07|N|123|53|49.68|E|type:landmark_region:JP-47|display=title}} == References == <references />

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:東御嶽 (竹富町新城上地)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:東御嶽 (竹富町新城上地)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:東御嶽 (竹富町新城上地)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q134963148]]Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:33, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:36, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{infobox religious building |name=Ārīugan }} {{Nihongo|'''Ārīugan'''|東御嶽}}<ref name="okinawastory">{{Cite web|ja | title = Aragusuku Island | url = https://www.okinawastory.jp/spot/1304 | work = Okinawa Story | publisher = Okinawa Convention & Visitors Bureau | accessdate = 2023-05-14 | archiveurl = https://web.archive.org/web/20221128013234/https://www.okinawastory.jp/spot/1304 | archivedate = 2022-11-28}}</ref> is an [[:en:Ontake|Utaki]] located on [[:en:Aragusuku Islands|Aragusuku Island]] (Kamiji Island), in [[:en:Taketomi, Okinawa|Taketomi, Okinawa]], [[:en:Okinawa Prefecture|Okinawa Prefecture]]. It is also known as '''Iso Utaki''' (磯御嶽, Ishāugan, Ishōugan), '''Gusuku Utaki''' (グスクウガン, Kūsukuugan<ref name="hesiki_1975">https://hdl.handle.net/2308/1372 </ref>), and '''Zannuon'''<ref name="shojiten">{{cite book|ja | title = Yaeyama Small Encyclopedia | page = 91 | author = Tsuneshin Sakihara | publisher = Border Ink | date = 1999-08-01}}</ref>. It is sometimes colloquially referred to as the "Mermaid Shrine" (人魚神社, Ningyo Jinja), though it is not a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]]<ref>{{cite encyclopedia|ja | title = Aragusuku Island | encyclopedia= Kadokawa Japan Place Name Encyclopedia 47 Okinawa Prefecture | author = Kadokawa Japan Place Name Compilation Committee | date= 1986-07-08 | publisher= Kadokawa Shoten | page = 130}}</ref>.
== Overview == Of the four Utaki on Kamiji Island, Higashi Utaki is the oldest and is regarded as the second holiest site after {{ill|Bi Utaki|ja|美御嶽|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bi Utaki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Naha-ugan). It is located east (Āru) of the only settlement on Kamiji Island and enshrines the deity of [[:en:Ryūgū-jō|Ryūgū]]<ref name="hesiki_1975" /><ref name="okinawastory" />. The site is situated on a rocky plateau, and behind the worship area is the remains of an enclosure surrounded by stone walls<ref name="shojiten" />. Fragments of {{ill|Panari pottery|ja|パナリ焼き|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Panari pottery|1=Panari ware|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11329177}} have been found around the site<ref name="shojiten" />.
Access to the Utaki is restricted, with a "No Entry" sign posted on the path leading to it. Only designated shrine caretakers are permitted to enter beyond the torii gate<ref name="okinawatimes_1999-12-18">{{cite news|ja | title = Island Life: Seafarers' Living (8) Aragusuku Island (Taketomi Town) | url = http://www.okinawatimes.co.jp/spe/yuku991218.html | newspaper = Okinawa Times | date = 1999-12-18 | archiveurl = https://web.archive.org/web/20000901000807/http://www.okinawatimes.co.jp/spe/yuku991218.html | archivedate = 2000-09-01}}</ref>.
== History == Higashi Utaki is also referred to as the "Original Utaki" (Mutuuugan), as it is believed to have been established when people first settled on Kamiji Island<ref name="hesiki_1975" />. Though the history of the island remains unclear, records from [[:en:1477|1477]] indicate that three shipwrecked Koreans from [[:en:Jeju Island|Jeju Island]] were rescued and repatriated through Aragusuku, confirming that the island had a settled population at that time. During the era when the [[:en:Ryukyu Kingdom|Ryukyu Kingdom]] imposed the [[:en:Poll tax|Head Tax]], Aragusuku Island, which had limited fresh water and farmland, was required to provide dried [[:en:Dugong|Dugong]] meat as tribute. The skulls of captured dugongs were offered at Higashi Utaki as part of prayers for abundant marine harvests. At one time, a large number of dugong skulls were piled at the site, but theft and other factors have significantly reduced their number. The name "Zannuon" means "Dugong Utaki," and "Iso" in "Iso Utaki" can also mean "sea".
== Rituals == Higashi Utaki is a focal point for prayers during the three major festivals of the island: the Harvest Festival (Buru), the Seasonal Festival (Siichi), and the Kichugan Festival (Kichugan). The {{ill|Kaminchu|ja|神司|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaminchu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (priestess) responsible for the shrine traditionally comes from the Asato family (Āru Asadei), while the {{ill|Kamanga|ja|カマンガー|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamanga|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (assistant male priest) comes from the Shin family (Āraei). As of 2013, the shrine has no serving priestess.
== See also ==

Access to the Utaki is restricted, with a "No Entry" sign posted on the path leading to it. Only designated shrine caretakers are permitted to enter beyond the torii gate<ref name="okinawatimes_1999-12-18">{{cite news|ja | title = Island Life: Seafarers' Living (8) Aragusuku Island (Taketomi Town) | url = http://www.okinawatimes.co.jp/spe/yuku991218.html | newspaper = Okinawa Times | date = 1999-12-18 | archiveurl = https://web.archive.org/web/20000901000807/http://www.okinawatimes.co.jp/spe/yuku991218.html | archivedate = 2000-09-01}}</ref>.

Only designated shrine caretakers are permitted to enter beyond the torii gate<ref name="okinawatimes_1999-12-18">{{cite news|ja | title = Island Life: Seafarers' Living (8) Aragusuku Island (Taketomi Town) | url = http://www.okinawatimes.co.jp/spe/yuku991218.html | newspaper = Okinawa Times | date = 1999-12-18 | archiveurl = https://web.archive.org/web/20000901000807/http://www.okinawatimes.co.jp/spe/yuku991218.html | archivedate = 2000-09-01}}</ref>.

Though the history of the island remains unclear, records from {{ill|1477|en|1477|WD=Q6614|lt=1477}} indicate that three shipwrecked Koreans from {{ill|Jeju Island|en|Jeju Island|WD=Q29151|lt=Jeju Island}} were rescued and repatriated through Aragusuku, confirming that the island had a settled population at that time. During the era when the {{ill|Ryukyu Kingdom|en|Ryukyu Kingdom|WD=Q28025|lt=Ryukyu Kingdom}} imposed the {{ill|Poll tax|en|Poll tax|WD=Q721368|lt=Head Tax}}, Aragusuku Island, which had limited fresh water and farmland, was required to provide dried {{ill|Dugong|en|Dugong|WD=Q129544|lt=Dugong}} meat as tribute. The skulls of captured dugongs were offered at Higashi Utaki as part of prayers for abundant marine harvests. At one time, a large number of dugong skulls were piled at the site, but theft and other factors have significantly reduced their number. The name "Zannuon" means "Dugong Utaki," and "Iso" in "Iso Utaki" can also mean "sea".

Only designated shrine caretakers are permitted to enter beyond the torii gate<ref name="okinawatimes_1999-12-18">{{cite news|ja | title = Island Life: Seafarers' Living (8) Aragusuku Island (Taketomi Town) | url = http://www.okinawatimes.co.jp/spe/yuku991218.html | newspaper = Okinawa Times | date = 1999-12-18 | archiveurl = https://web.archive.org/web/20000901000807/http://www.okinawatimes.co.jp/spe/yuku991218.html | archivedate = 2000-09-01}}</ref>.
== History == Higashi Utaki is also referred to as the "Original Utaki" (Mutuuugan), as it is believed to have been established when people first settled on Kamiji Island<ref name="hesiki_1975" />. Though the history of the island remains unclear, records from {{ill|1477|en|1477|WD=Q6614|lt=1477}} indicate that three shipwrecked Koreans from {{ill|Jeju Island|en|Jeju Island|WD=Q29151|lt=Jeju Island}} were rescued and repatriated through Aragusuku, confirming that the island had a settled population at that time. During the era when the {{ill|Ryukyu Kingdom|en|Ryukyu Kingdom|WD=Q28025|lt=Ryukyu Kingdom}} imposed the {{ill|Poll tax|en|Poll tax|WD=Q721368|lt=Head Tax}}, Aragusuku Island, which had limited fresh water and farmland, was required to provide dried {{ill|Dugong|en|Dugong|WD=Q129544|lt=Dugong}} meat as tribute. The skulls of captured dugongs were offered at Higashi Utaki as part of prayers for abundant marine harvests. At one time, a large number of dugong skulls were piled at the site, but theft and other factors have significantly reduced their number. The name "Zannuon" means "Dugong Utaki," and "Iso" in "Iso Utaki" can also mean "sea".
== Rituals == Higashi Utaki is a focal point for prayers during the three major festivals of the island: the Harvest Festival (Buru), the Seasonal Festival (Siichi), and the Kichugan Festival (Kichugan). The {{ill|Kaminchu|ja|神司|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaminchu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (priestess) responsible for the shrine traditionally comes from the Asato family (Āru Asadei), while the {{ill|Kamanga|ja|カマンガー|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamanga|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (assistant male priest) comes from the Shin family (Āraei). As of 2013, the shrine has no serving priestess.
== See also ==
* {{ill|Akamata and Kuromata|ja|アカマタ・クロマタ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akamata and Kuromata|1=Akamata and Kuromata|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11282723}} {{coord|24|18|15.07|N|123|53|49.68|E|type:landmark_region:JP-47|display=title}} == References == <references />

[[ja:東御嶽 (竹富町新城上地)]] [[sn:Ārīugan]] [[ja:Ārīugan]] {{wikidata link|Q134963148}} [[Category:Buildings in Taketomi Town]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:History of Taketomi Town]] [[Category:Utaki (Okinawa)]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:東御嶽 (竹富町新城上地)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:東御嶽 (竹富町新城上地)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:東御嶽 (竹富町新城上地)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q134963148]]Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:33, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:36, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{infobox religious building |name=Ārīugan }} {{Nihongo|'''Ārīugan'''|東御嶽}}<ref name="okinawastory">{{Cite web|ja | title = Aragusuku Island | url = https://www.okinawastory.jp/spot/1304 | work = Okinawa Story | publisher = Okinawa Convention & Visitors Bureau | accessdate = 2023-05-14 | archiveurl = https://web.archive.org/web/20221128013234/https://www.okinawastory.jp/spot/1304 | archivedate = 2022-11-28}}</ref> is an {{ill|Ontake|en|Ontake|WD=Q11071176|lt=Utaki}} located on {{ill|Aragusuku Islands|en|Aragusuku Islands|WD=Q702414|lt=Aragusuku Island}} (Kamiji Island), in {{ill|Taketomi, Okinawa|en|Taketomi, Okinawa|WD=Q699764|lt=Taketomi, Okinawa}}, {{ill|Okinawa Prefecture|en|Okinawa Prefecture|WD=Q766445|lt=Okinawa Prefecture}}. It is also known as '''Iso Utaki''' (磯御嶽, Ishāugan, Ishōugan), '''Gusuku Utaki''' (グスクウガン, Kūsukuugan<ref name="hesiki_1975">https://hdl.handle.net/2308/1372 </ref>), and '''Zannuon'''<ref name="shojiten">{{cite book|ja | title = Yaeyama Small Encyclopedia | page = 91 | author = Tsuneshin Sakihara | publisher = Border Ink | date = 1999-08-01}}</ref>. It is sometimes colloquially referred to as the "Mermaid Shrine" (人魚神社, Ningyo Jinja), though it is not a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}}<ref>{{cite encyclopedia|ja | title = Aragusuku Island | encyclopedia= Kadokawa Japan Place Name Encyclopedia 47 Okinawa Prefecture | author = Kadokawa Japan Place Name Compilation Committee | date= 1986-07-08 | publisher= Kadokawa Shoten | page = 130}}</ref>.
== Overview == Of the four Utaki on Kamiji Island, Higashi Utaki is the oldest and is regarded as the second holiest site after {{ill|Bi Utaki|ja|美御嶽|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bi Utaki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Naha-ugan). It is located east (Āru) of the only settlement on Kamiji Island and enshrines the deity of {{ill|Ryūgū-jō|en|Ryūgū-jō|WD=Q1201828|lt=Ryūgū}}<ref name="hesiki_1975" /><ref name="okinawastory" />. The site is situated on a rocky plateau, and behind the worship area is the remains of an enclosure surrounded by stone walls<ref name="shojiten" />. Fragments of {{ill|Panari pottery|ja|パナリ焼き|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Panari pottery|1=Panari ware|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11329177}} have been found around the site<ref name="shojiten" />.
Access to the Utaki is restricted, with a "No Entry" sign posted on the path leading to it. Only designated shrine caretakers are permitted to enter beyond the torii gate<ref name="okinawatimes_1999-12-18">{{cite news|ja | title = Island Life: Seafarers' Living (8) Aragusuku Island (Taketomi Town) | url = http://www.okinawatimes.co.jp/spe/yuku991218.html | newspaper = Okinawa Times | date = 1999-12-18 | archiveurl = https://web.archive.org/web/20000901000807/http://www.okinawatimes.co.jp/spe/yuku991218.html | archivedate = 2000-09-01}}</ref>.
== History == Higashi Utaki is also referred to as the "Original Utaki" (Mutuuugan), as it is believed to have been established when people first settled on Kamiji Island<ref name="hesiki_1975" />. Though the history of the island remains unclear, records from {{ill|1477|en|1477|WD=Q6614|lt=1477}} indicate that three shipwrecked Koreans from {{ill|Jeju Island|en|Jeju Island|WD=Q29151|lt=Jeju Island}} were rescued and repatriated through Aragusuku, confirming that the island had a settled population at that time. During the era when the {{ill|Ryukyu Kingdom|en|Ryukyu Kingdom|WD=Q28025|lt=Ryukyu Kingdom}} imposed the {{ill|Poll tax|en|Poll tax|WD=Q721368|lt=Head Tax}}, Aragusuku Island, which had limited fresh water and farmland, was required to provide dried {{ill|Dugong|en|Dugong|WD=Q129544|lt=Dugong}} meat as tribute. The skulls of captured dugongs were offered at Higashi Utaki as part of prayers for abundant marine harvests. At one time, a large number of dugong skulls were piled at the site, but theft and other factors have significantly reduced their number. The name "Zannuon" means "Dugong Utaki," and "Iso" in "Iso Utaki" can also mean "sea".
== Rituals == Higashi Utaki is a focal point for prayers during the three major festivals of the island: the Harvest Festival (Buru), the Seasonal Festival (Siichi), and the Kichugan Festival (Kichugan). The {{ill|Kaminchu|ja|神司|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaminchu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (priestess) responsible for the shrine traditionally comes from the Asato family (Āru Asadei), while the {{ill|Kamanga|ja|カマンガー|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamanga|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (assistant male priest) comes from the Shin family (Āraei). As of 2013, the shrine has no serving priestess.

{{infobox religious building |name=Ārīugan }} {{Nihongo|'''Ārīugan'''|東御嶽}}<ref name="okinawastory">{{Cite web|ja | title = Aragusuku Island | url = https://www.okinawastory.jp/spot/1304 | work = Okinawa Story | publisher = Okinawa Convention & Visitors Bureau | accessdate = 2023-05-14 | archiveurl = https://web.archive.org/web/20221128013234/https://www.okinawastory.jp/spot/1304 | archivedate = 2022-11-28}}</ref> is an {{ill|Ontake|en|Ontake|WD=Q11071176|lt=Utaki|1=Ontake}} located on {{ill|Aragusuku Islands|en|Aragusuku Islands|WD=Q702414|lt=Aragusuku Island|1=Aragusuku Islands}} (Kamiji Island), in {{ill|Taketomi, Okinawa|en|Taketomi, Okinawa|WD=Q699764|lt=Taketomi, Okinawa|1=Taketomi, Okinawa}}, {{ill|Okinawa Prefecture|en|Okinawa Prefecture|WD=Q766445|lt=Okinawa Prefecture|1=Okinawa Prefecture}}. It is also known as '''Iso Utaki''' (磯御嶽, Ishāugan, Ishōugan), '''Gusuku Utaki''' (グスクウガン, Kūsukuugan<ref name="hesiki_1975">https://hdl.handle.net/2308/1372 </ref>), and '''Zannuon'''<ref name="shojiten">{{cite book|ja | title = Yaeyama Small Encyclopedia | page = 91 | author = Tsuneshin Sakihara | publisher = Border Ink | date = 1999-08-01}}</ref>. It is sometimes colloquially referred to as the "Mermaid Shrine" (人魚神社, Ningyo Jinja), though it is not a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}}<ref>{{cite encyclopedia|ja | title = Aragusuku Island | encyclopedia= Kadokawa Japan Place Name Encyclopedia 47 Okinawa Prefecture | author = Kadokawa Japan Place Name Compilation Committee | date= 1986-07-08 | publisher= Kadokawa Shoten | page = 130}}</ref>.
== Overview == Of the four Utaki on Kamiji Island, Higashi Utaki is the oldest and is regarded as the second holiest site after {{ill|Bi Utaki|ja|美御嶽|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bi Utaki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Naha-ugan). It is located east (Āru) of the only settlement on Kamiji Island and enshrines the deity of {{ill|Ryūgū-jō|en|Ryūgū-jō|WD=Q1201828|lt=Ryūgū|1=Ryūgū-jō}}<ref name="hesiki_1975" /><ref name="okinawastory" />. The site is situated on a rocky plateau, and behind the worship area is the remains of an enclosure surrounded by stone walls<ref name="shojiten" />. Fragments of {{ill|Panari pottery|ja|パナリ焼き|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Panari pottery|1=Panari ware|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11329177}} have been found around the site<ref name="shojiten" />.
Access to the Utaki is restricted, with a "No Entry" sign posted on the path leading to it. Only designated shrine caretakers are permitted to enter beyond the torii gate<ref name="okinawatimes_1999-12-18">{{cite news|ja | title = Island Life: Seafarers' Living (8) Aragusuku Island (Taketomi Town) | url = http://www.okinawatimes.co.jp/spe/yuku991218.html | newspaper = Okinawa Times | date = 1999-12-18 | archiveurl = https://web.archive.org/web/20000901000807/http://www.okinawatimes.co.jp/spe/yuku991218.html | archivedate = 2000-09-01}}</ref>.
== History == Higashi Utaki is also referred to as the "Original Utaki" (Mutuuugan), as it is believed to have been established when people first settled on Kamiji Island<ref name="hesiki_1975" />. Though the history of the island remains unclear, records from {{ill|1477|en|1477|WD=Q6614|lt=1477|1=1477}} indicate that three shipwrecked Koreans from {{ill|Jeju Island|en|Jeju Island|WD=Q29151|lt=Jeju Island|1=Jeju Island}} were rescued and repatriated through Aragusuku, confirming that the island had a settled population at that time. During the era when the {{ill|Ryukyu Kingdom|en|Ryukyu Kingdom|WD=Q28025|lt=Ryukyu Kingdom|1=Ryukyu Kingdom}} imposed the {{ill|Poll tax|en|Poll tax|WD=Q721368|lt=Head Tax|1=Poll tax}}, Aragusuku Island, which had limited fresh water and farmland, was required to provide dried {{ill|Dugong|en|Dugong|WD=Q129544|lt=Dugong|1=Dugong}} meat as tribute. The skulls of captured dugongs were offered at Higashi Utaki as part of prayers for abundant marine harvests. At one time, a large number of dugong skulls were piled at the site, but theft and other factors have significantly reduced their number. The name "Zannuon" means "Dugong Utaki," and "Iso" in "Iso Utaki" can also mean "sea".
== Rituals == Higashi Utaki is a focal point for prayers during the three major festivals of the island: the Harvest Festival (Buru), the Seasonal Festival (Siichi), and the Kichugan Festival (Kichugan). The {{ill|Kaminchu|ja|神司|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaminchu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (priestess) responsible for the shrine traditionally comes from the Asato family (Āru Asadei), while the {{ill|Kamanga|ja|カマンガー|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamanga|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (assistant male priest) comes from the Shin family (Āraei). As of 2013, the shrine has no serving priestess.

* {{ill|Akamata and Kuromata|ja|アカマタ・クロマタ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akamata and Kuromata|1=Akamata and Kuromata|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11282723}} {{coord|24|18|15.07|N|123|53|49.68|E|type:landmark_region:JP-47|display=title}} == References == <references />
== Bibliography ==

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:東御嶽 (竹富町新城上地)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:東御嶽 (竹富町新城上地)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:東御嶽 (竹富町新城上地)]]
{{infobox religious building |name=Ārīugan }} {{Nihongo|'''Ārīugan'''|東御嶽}}<ref name="okinawastory">{{Cite web|ja | title = Aragusuku Island | url = https://www.okinawastory.jp/spot/1304 | work = Okinawa Story | publisher = Okinawa Convention & Visitors Bureau | accessdate = 2023-05-14 | archiveurl = https://web.archive.org/web/20221128013234/https://www.okinawastory.jp/spot/1304 | archivedate = 2022-11-28}}</ref> is an {{ill|Ontake|en|Ontake|WD=Q11071176|lt=Utaki|1=Ontake}} located on {{ill|Aragusuku Islands|en|Aragusuku Islands|WD=Q702414|lt=Aragusuku Island|1=Aragusuku Islands}} (Kamiji Island), in {{ill|Taketomi, Okinawa|en|Taketomi, Okinawa|WD=Q699764|lt=Taketomi, Okinawa|1=Taketomi, Okinawa}}, {{ill|Okinawa Prefecture|en|Okinawa Prefecture|WD=Q766445|lt=Okinawa Prefecture|1=Okinawa Prefecture}}. It is also known as '''Iso Utaki''' (磯御嶽, Ishāugan, Ishōugan), '''Gusuku Utaki''' (グスクウガン, Kūsukuugan<ref name="hesiki_1975">https://hdl.handle.net/2308/1372 </ref>), and '''Zannuon'''<ref name="shojiten">{{cite book|ja | title = Yaeyama Small Encyclopedia | page = 91 | author = Tsuneshin Sakihara | publisher = Border Ink | date = 1999-08-01}}</ref>. It is sometimes colloquially referred to as the "Mermaid Shrine" (人魚神社, Ningyo Jinja), though it is not a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}}<ref>{{cite encyclopedia|ja | title = Aragusuku Island | encyclopedia= Kadokawa Japan Place Name Encyclopedia 47 Okinawa Prefecture | author = Kadokawa Japan Place Name Compilation Committee | date= 1986-07-08 | publisher= Kadokawa Shoten | page = 130}}</ref>.
== Overview == Of the four Utaki on Kamiji Island, Higashi Utaki is the oldest and is regarded as the second holiest site after {{ill|Bi Utaki|ja|美御嶽|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bi Utaki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Naha-ugan). It is located east (Āru) of the only settlement on Kamiji Island and enshrines the deity of {{ill|Ryūgū-jō|en|Ryūgū-jō|WD=Q1201828|lt=Ryūgū|1=Ryūgū-jō}}<ref name="hesiki_1975" /><ref name="okinawastory" />. The site is situated on a rocky plateau, and behind the worship area is the remains of an enclosure surrounded by stone walls<ref name="shojiten" />. Fragments of {{ill|Panari pottery|ja|パナリ焼き|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Panari pottery|1=Panari ware|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11329177}} have been found around the site<ref name="shojiten" />.
Access to the Utaki is restricted, with a "No Entry" sign posted on the path leading to it. Only designated shrine caretakers are permitted to enter beyond the torii gate<ref name="okinawatimes_1999-12-18">{{cite news|ja | title = Island Life: Seafarers' Living (8) Aragusuku Island (Taketomi Town) | url = http://www.okinawatimes.co.jp/spe/yuku991218.html | newspaper = Okinawa Times | date = 1999-12-18 | archiveurl = https://web.archive.org/web/20000901000807/http://www.okinawatimes.co.jp/spe/yuku991218.html | archivedate = 2000-09-01}}</ref>.
== History == Higashi Utaki is also referred to as the "Original Utaki" (Mutuuugan), as it is believed to have been established when people first settled on Kamiji Island<ref name="hesiki_1975" />. Though the history of the island remains unclear, records from {{ill|1477|en|1477|WD=Q6614|lt=1477|1=1477}} indicate that three shipwrecked Koreans from {{ill|Jeju Island|en|Jeju Island|WD=Q29151|lt=Jeju Island|1=Jeju Island}} were rescued and repatriated through Aragusuku, confirming that the island had a settled population at that time. During the era when the {{ill|Ryukyu Kingdom|en|Ryukyu Kingdom|WD=Q28025|lt=Ryukyu Kingdom|1=Ryukyu Kingdom}} imposed the {{ill|Poll tax|en|Poll tax|WD=Q721368|lt=Head Tax|1=Poll tax}}, Aragusuku Island, which had limited fresh water and farmland, was required to provide dried {{ill|Dugong|en|Dugong|WD=Q129544|lt=Dugong|1=Dugong}} meat as tribute. The skulls of captured dugongs were offered at Higashi Utaki as part of prayers for abundant marine harvests. At one time, a large number of dugong skulls were piled at the site, but theft and other factors have significantly reduced their number. The name "Zannuon" means "Dugong Utaki," and "Iso" in "Iso Utaki" can also mean "sea".
== Rituals == Higashi Utaki is a focal point for prayers during the three major festivals of the island: the Harvest Festival (Buru), the Seasonal Festival (Siichi), and the Kichugan Festival (Kichugan). The {{ill|Kaminchu|ja|神司|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaminchu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (priestess) responsible for the shrine traditionally comes from the Asato family (Āru Asadei), while the {{ill|Kamanga|ja|カマンガー|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamanga|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (assistant male priest) comes from the Shin family (Āraei). As of 2013, the shrine has no serving priestess.
== See also ==
* {{ill|Akamata and Kuromata|ja|アカマタ・クロマタ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akamata and Kuromata|1=Akamata and Kuromata|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11282723}} {{coord|24|18|15.07|N|123|53|49.68|E|type:landmark_region:JP-47|display=title}} == References == <references />
== Bibliography ==
* {{cite book|ja|title=Taketomi Town History Volume 5: Aragusuku Island|editor=Taketomi Town History Compilation Committee|date=2013-11-30|ref={{SfnRef|町史|2013}}}}
